functions.name: GMImage.AdaptiveThreshold
functions.description: Local adaptive threshold image.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: defines the size of the pixel neighborhood

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: defines the size of the pixel neighborhood

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Optional, constant to subtract from pixel neighborhood mean

functions.name: GMImage.AddNoise
functions.description: Add noise to image with specified noise type.

parameters.name: noiseType
parameters.description: UniformNoise = 0, GaussianNoise = 1, MultiplicativeGaussianNoise = 2, ImpulseNoise = 3, LaplacianNoise = 4, PoissonNoise = 5.

functions.name: GMImage.AddNoiseChannel
functions.description: Add noise to image with specified noise type.

parameters.name: ChannelType
parameters.description: One of the following values: RedChannel = 1 (RGB Red channel), CyanChannel = 2 (CMYK Cyan channel), GreenChannel = 3 (RGB Green channel), MagentaChannel = 4 (CMYK Magenta channel), BlueChannel = 5 (RGB Blue channel), YellowChannel = 6 (CMYK Yellow channel), OpacityChannel = 7 (Opacity channel), BlackChannel = 8 (CMYK Black (K) channel), MatteChannel = 9 (Same as Opacity channel (deprecated)), AllChannels = 10 (Color channels), GrayChannel = 11 (Color channels represent an intensity.)

parameters.name: noiseType
parameters.description: UniformNoise = 0, GaussianNoise = 1, MultiplicativeGaussianNoise = 2, ImpulseNoise = 3, LaplacianNoise = 4, PoissonNoise = 5.

functions.name: GMImage.AddPathArcAbs
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.AddPathArcAbs"; ImageRef; radiusX; radiusY; xAxisRotation; largeArcFlag; sweepFlag; X; Y )
functions.description: Draw a single arc segment. Draws an elliptical arc from the current point to (x, y). The size and orientation of the ellipse are defined by two radii (radiusX, radiusY) and an xAxisRotation, which indicates how the ellipse as a whole is rotated relative to the current coordinate system. The center (cx, cy) of the ellipse is calculated automatically to satisfy the constraints imposed by the other parameters. largeArcFlag and sweepFlag contribute to the automatic calculations and help determine how the arc is drawn. If largeArcFlag is 1 then draw the larger of the available arcs. If sweepFlag is 1, then draw the arc matching a clock-wise rotation. With absolute coordinate.

parameters.name: largeArcFlag
parameters.description: Pass 1 or 0 to decide if you want to draw larger arcs.

parameters.name: sweepFlag
parameters.description: Whether to draw the arc matching a clock-wise rotation.

functions.name: GMImage.AddPathArcRel
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.AddPathArcRel"; ImageRef; radiusX; radiusY; xAxisRotation; largeArcFlag; sweepFlag; X; Y )
functions.description: Draw a single arc segment. Draws an elliptical arc from the current point to (x, y). The size and orientation of the ellipse are defined by two radii (radiusX, radiusY) and an xAxisRotation, which indicates how the ellipse as a whole is rotated relative to the current coordinate system. The center (cx, cy) of the ellipse is calculated automatically to satisfy the constraints imposed by the other parameters. largeArcFlag and sweepFlag contribute to the automatic calculations and help determine how the arc is drawn. If largeArcFlag is 1 then draw the larger of the available arcs. If sweepFlag is 1, then draw the arc matching a clock-wise rotation. With relative coordinate.

parameters.name: largeArcFlag
parameters.description: Pass 1 or 0 to decide if you want to draw larger arcs.

parameters.name: sweepFlag
parameters.description: Whether to draw the arc matching a clock-wise rotation.

functions.name: GMImage.AddPathClosePath
functions.description: Closes subpath. Close the current subpath by drawing a straight line from the current point to current subpath's most recent starting point (usually, the most recent moveto point).

functions.name: GMImage.AddPathCurvetoAbs
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.AddPathCurvetoAbs"; ImageRef; x1; y1; x2; y2; X; Y )
functions.description: Draw a single curve. Draws a cubic Bézier curve from the current point to (x,*y*) using (x1,*y1*) as the control point at the beginning of the curve and (x2,*y2*) as the control point at the end of the curve. GMImage.AddPathCurvetoAbs indicates that absolutecoordinates will follow; GMImage.AddPathCurvetoRel indicates that relative coordinates will follow. Multiple sets of coordinates may be specified to draw a polybezier. At the end of the command, the new current point becomes the final (x,*y*) coordinate pair used in the polybezier.

examples.label: Draws a bezier curve:
examples.text_code:
$r
=
MBS
("
GMImage.AddPathMovetoAbs
";
$img
; 100; 100 )
$r
=
MBS
("GMImage.AddPathCurvetoAbs";
$img
; 100; 300; 300; 100; 300; 300 )
$r
=
MBS
("
GMImage.DrawPath
";
$img
)
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.AddPathMovetoAbs"; $img; 100; 100 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.AddPathCurvetoAbs"; $img; 100; 300; 300; 100; 300; 300 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.DrawPath"; $img )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.AddPathCurvetoRel
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.AddPathCurvetoRel"; ImageRef; x1; y1; x2; y2; X; Y )
functions.description: Draw a single curve. Draws a cubic Bézier curve from the current point to (x,*y*) using (x1,*y1*) as the control point at the beginning of the curve and (x2,*y2*) as the control point at the end of the curve. GMImage.AddPathCurvetoAbs indicates that absolutecoordinates will follow; GMImage.AddPathCurvetoRel indicates that relative coordinates will follow. Multiple sets of coordinates may be specified to draw a polybezier. At the end of the command, the new current point becomes the final (x,*y*) coordinate pair used in the polybezier.

functions.name: GMImage.AddPathLinetoAbs
functions.description: Draw to a single point. Draw a line from the current point to the given coordinate which becomes the new current point. Relative coordinates are used. At the end of the command, the new current point is set to the final set of coordinates provided.

functions.name: GMImage.AddPathLinetoHorizontalAbs
functions.description: Draws a horizontal line from the current point (cpx, cpy) to (x, cpy). GMImage.AddPathLinetoHorizontalAbs indicates that absolute coordinates are supplied. At the end of the command, the new current point becomes (x, cpy) for the final value of x

functions.name: GMImage.AddPathLinetoHorizontalRel
functions.description: Draws a horizontal line from the current point (cpx, cpy) to (x, cpy). GMImage.AddPathLinetoHorizontalRel indicates that relative coordinates are supplied. At the end of the command, the new current point becomes (x, cpy) for the final value of x.

functions.name: GMImage.AddPathLinetoRel
functions.description: Draw to a single point. Draw a line from the current point to the given coordinate which becomes the new current point. Relative coordinates are used. At the end of the command, the new current point is set to the final set of coordinates provided.

functions.name: GMImage.AddPathLinetoVerticalAbs
functions.description: Draws a vertical line from the current point (cpx, cpy) to (cpx, y). PathLinetoVerticalAbs indicates that absolute coordinates are supplied. At the end of the command, the new current point becomes (cpx, y) for the final value of y.

functions.name: GMImage.AddPathLinetoVerticalRel
functions.description: Draws a vertical line from the current point (cpx, cpy) to (cpx, y). GMImage.AddPathLinetoVerticalRel indicates that relative coordinates are supplied. At the end of the command, the new current point becomes (cpx, y) for the final value of y.

functions.name: GMImage.AddPathMovetoAbs
functions.description: Simple moveto with absolute coordinate.

functions.name: GMImage.AddPathMovetoRel
functions.description: Simple moveto with relative coordinate.

functions.name: GMImage.AddPathQuadraticCurvetoRel

functions.name: GMImage.AddPathSmoothCurvetoAbs
functions.description: Adds a smooth curve to path.

functions.name: GMImage.AddPathSmoothCurvetoRel
functions.description: Draw a single curve.

functions.name: GMImage.AddPathSmoothQuadraticCurvetoAbs
functions.description: Adds smooth quadratic curve to relative point to current path.

functions.name: GMImage.AddPathSmoothQuadraticCurvetoRel
functions.description: Adds smooth quadratic curve to relative point to current path.

functions.name: GMImage.Affine
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.Affine"; ImageRef { ; sx; sy; rx; ry; tx; ty } )
functions.description: Applies an affine transformation. Specify a transformation matrix to adjust scaling, rotation, and translation (coordinate transformation) for subsequently drawn objects in the same or decendent drawing context. The sx & sy parameters represent the x & y scale factors, the rx & ry parameters represent the x & y rotation, and the tx & ty parameters represent the x & y translation:

parameters.name: sx
parameters.description: The x scale factors. Default is 1.

parameters.name: sy
parameters.description: The y scale factors. Default is 1.

functions.name: GMImage.AffineTransform
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.AffineTransform"; ImageRef { ; sx; sy; rx; ry; tx; ty } )
functions.description: Transform image by specified affine (or free transform) matrix.

parameters.name: sx
parameters.description: The x scale factors. Default is 1.

parameters.name: sy
parameters.description: The y scale factors. Default is 1.

functions.name: GMImage.Annotate
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.Annotate"; ImageRef; Text { ; boundingArea; gravity; degrees } )
functions.description: Annotate image (draw text on image). Annotates an image with text. Optionally you can include any of the following bits of information about the image by embedding the appropriate special characters: %b file size in bytes. %c comment. %d directory in which the image resides. %e extension of the image file. %f original filename of the image. %h height of image. %i filename of the image. %k number of unique colors. %l image label. %m image file format. %n number of images in a image sequence. %o output image filename. %p page number of the image. %q image depth (8 or 16). %p page number of the image. %q image depth (8 or 16). %s image scene number. %t image filename without any extension. %u a unique temporary filename. %w image width. %x x resolution of the image. %y y resolution of the image. Set a font with full path and @ in front. e.g. "@/Library/Fonts/Arial.ttf". This way the plugin loads it directly. Gravity values:

parameters.name: boundingArea
parameters.description: The rectangle where to draw. Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200". With an optional offset "100x200+10+20". For percent values, add a percent on the end, for % values add a %: "100x200%!"

parameters.name: gravity
parameters.description: The position of the text. Can be number of text value. Can be ForgetGravity=0, NorthWestGravity=1, NorthGravity=2, NorthEastGravity=3, WestGravity=4, CenterGravity=5, EastGravity=6, SouthWestGravity=7, SouthGravity=8, SouthEastGravity=9, StaticGravity=10.

examples.label: Write three lines at 200/200 into the picture:

( "GMImage.Annotate";
$ImageRef
; "Hello\nWorld\nAgain"; "200x200"; 0; 0 )

examples.label: Load pictures, sets puts text on it in yellow and centered:
examples.text_code:
# load image from container
Set Variable [
$ref
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Annotate Image::InputImage
)]
# set font and font size
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetFontPointSize
";
$ref
;
Annotate Image::FontSize
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetFont
";
$ref
;
Annotate Image::FontPath
)]
# set color to yellow
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetFillColor
";
$ref
; "RGB 1 1 0")]
# draw in rectangle with width and height of image (here 640 by 480) centered.
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("GMImage.Annotate";
$ref
;
Annotate Image::Text
; "640x480"; "CenterGravity")]
# save image
Set Field [
Annotate Image::OutputImage
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
";
$ref
)]
# and free memory
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$ref
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> load image from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; Annotate Image::InputImage)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ref</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set font and font size</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetFontPointsize"; $ref; Annotate Image::FontSize)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetFont"; $ref; Annotate Image::FontPath)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set color to yellow</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetFillColor"; $ref; "RGB 1 1 0")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> draw in rectangle with width and height of image (here 640 by 480) centered.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Annotate"; $ref; Annotate Image::Text; "640x480"; "CenterGravity")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> save image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $ref )</Calculation>
    <Field table="Annotate Image" id="99999" name="OutputImage"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and free memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMimage.Release";$ref)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Error</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.AutoOrient
functions.description: Orient image to be right-side up based on its current orientation attribute. This allows the image to be viewed correctly when the orientation attribute is not available, or is not respected.

examples.label: Try it:
examples.text_code:
# load image
Set Variable [
$Image
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Correct Image Orientation::Input
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
#
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.AutoOrient";
$Image
) ]
#
#  Write back to new field
Set Field [
Correct Image Orientation::Output
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer
";
$Image
; GetAsText(
Correct Image Orientation::Input
)) ]
#
# free memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Free
";
$Image
) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> load image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.NewFromContainer"; Correct Image Orientation::Input ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.AutoOrient"; $Image ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>  Write back to new field</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer"; $Image; GetAsText(Correct Image Orientation::Input)) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Correct Image Orientation" id="99999" name="Output "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> free memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.Free"; $Image) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.AveragePixelValue
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.AveragePixelValue"; ImageRef { ; X; Y; Width; Height } )
functions.description: Calculates the average pixel value. Value is in range from 0.0 to 1.0 depending if it is more black (0.0) or white (1.0). Make sure the pixels are in RGB format before you call this method. (Use GMImage.SetType with value 6.) This function is designed to test if a certain area in the image is white or black. Does not check alpha channel! Version 9.1 and later uses full image if rectangle is missed.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The width of the rectangle. Default is width of image.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: The height of the rectangle. Default is height of image.

functions.name: GMImage.BaseColumns
functions.description: Returns the base image width (before transformations).
functions.result: Returns a number for the base image width (before transformations).

functions.name: GMImage.BaseFilename
functions.description: Returns the Base image filename (before transformations)

functions.name: GMImage.BaseRows
functions.description: Returns the base image height (before transformations) This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 GMImage.BaseFilename - GMImage.Blur 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: GMImage.Blur
functions.description: Blur image with specified blur factor.

parameters.name: Radius
parameters.description: Optional, The radius of the Gaussian, in pixels, not counting the center pixel.

parameters.name: sigma
parameters.description: Optional, The standard deviation of the Laplacian, in pixels.

examples.label: Blurs image
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( 
[ 
$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$RotateResult = MBS("GMImage.Blur"; $Image; 10; 5); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $Image); 
$DestroyResult = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 
$Result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.BlurChannel
functions.description: Blur image with specified blur factor.

parameters.name: ChannelType
parameters.description: One of the following values: RedChannel = 1 (RGB Red channel), CyanChannel = 2 (CMYK Cyan channel), GreenChannel = 3 (RGB Green channel), MagentaChannel = 4 (CMYK Magenta channel), BlueChannel = 5 (RGB Blue channel), YellowChannel = 6 (CMYK Yellow channel), OpacityChannel = 7 (Opacity channel), BlackChannel = 8 (CMYK Black (K) channel), MatteChannel = 9 (Same as Opacity channel (deprecated)), AllChannels = 10 (Color channels), GrayChannel = 11 (Color channels represent an intensity.)

parameters.name: Radius
parameters.description: The radius of the Gaussian, in pixels, not counting the center pixel.

parameters.name: sigma
parameters.description: The standard deviation of the Laplacian, in pixels.

functions.name: GMImage.Border
functions.description: Border image (add border to image). The color of the border is specified by the borderColor attribute. Offset from geometry is not used, just width and height.

parameters.name: Geometry
parameters.description: Optional, The rectangle with border width and height. Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200".

examples.label: Scale image down, add border and crop:
examples.text_code:
# Load from container
Set Variable [
$Image
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Image Scaling::InputImage
) ]
#
# Scale the image by the size needed
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Scale
";
$Image
; "80x200") ]
#
# calculate border to add
Set Variable [
$w
; Value: Round ( (80 -
MBS
("
GMImage.GetWidth
";
$Image
)) / 2 ; 0 ) ]
Set Variable [
$h
; Value: Round ( (200 -
MBS
("
GMImage.GetHeight
";
$Image
)) / 2 ; 0 ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetBorderColor
";
$Image
; "white") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("GMImage.Border";
$Image
;
$w
& "x" &
$h
) ]
#
# cut to make sure we are not off by one extra pixel
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Crop
";
$Image
; "80x200") ]
#
# Write image to container and set the Information text
Set Field [
Image Scaling::Result
;
MBS
("
GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
";
$Image
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Free
";
$Image
) ]

functions.name: GMImage.BoundingBox

functions.result: Returns a string with a geometry.

functions.name: GMImage.ClipMask
functions.description: Associate a clip mask with the image. The clip mask must be the same dimensions as the image. Pass an invalid image to unset an existing clip mask. Returns an error in case there is no clip mask.

functions.name: GMImage.ClipPath
functions.description: Select a drawing clip path matching name.

functions.name: GMImage.Coders
functions.description: Lists all coders as JSON. For each coder, we have the following entries:

parameters.name: MultiFrame
parameters.description: Coder may read/write multiple frames (default True)

functions.name: GMImage.Colorize
functions.description: Colorize image with pen color, using specified percent opacity. Blends the fill color with each pixel in the image. A percentage blend is specified with opacity.  Control the application of different color components by specifying a different percentage for each component (e.g. 90/100/10 is 90% red, 100% green, and 10% blue).

parameters.name: penColor
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

examples.label: Colorize an image:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$image
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
test::Input
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.Colorize";
$image
; 50; "RGB 1 0 0" ) ]
Set Field [
test::Output
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
";
$image
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Release
";
$image
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.NewFromContainer"; test::Input ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.Colorize"; $image; 50; "RGB 1 0 0" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $image) </Calculation>
    <Field table="test" id="99999" name="Output "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.Release"; $image) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.ColorizeRGB
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.ColorizeRGB"; ImageRef; opacityRed; opacityGreen; opacityBlue; penColor )
functions.description: Colorize image with pen color, using specified percent opacity for red, green, and blue quantums. Blends the fill color with each pixel in the image. A percentage blend is specified with opacity.  Control the application of different color components by specifying a different percentage for each component (e.g. 90/100/10 is 90% red, 100% green, and 10% blue).

parameters.name: penColor
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

examples.label: Colorize an image

("GMImage.ColorizeRGB";
$BigImageRef
; 10; 0; 0; "green")

functions.name: GMImage.Columns
functions.description: Returns the Image width.

functions.name: GMImage.Compare
functions.description: Compares current image with another image. Sets meanErrorPerPixel, normalizedMaxError, and normalizedMeanError in the current image. False is returned if the images are identical.
functions.result: Returns true if equal and false if not equal.

examples.label: Compare images:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$img1
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
test::Container1
)]
Set Variable [
$img2
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
test::Container2
)]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.Compare";
$img1
;
$img2
)]
If [
$result
= 1]
Show Custom Dialog ["Image Comparison"; "equal"]
Else If [
$result
= 0]
Set Variable [
$e
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.NormalizedMaxError
";
$img1
)]
If [
$e
≠ 0 and
$e
< ,03]
Show Custom Dialog ["Image Comparison"; "very similar: " &
MBS
( "
GMImage.NormalizedMaxError
";
$img1
)]
Else
Show Custom Dialog ["Image Comparison"; "not equal"]
End If
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$img1
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$img2
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.NewFromContainer"; test::Container1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$img1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.NewFromContainer"; test::Container2 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$img2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.Compare"; $img1; $img2 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$result = 1</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Image Comparison"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"equal"</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="125" name="Else If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$result = 0</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.NormalizedMaxError"; $img1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$e</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$e ≠ 0 and $e < ,03</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Image Comparison"; "very similar: " & MBS( "GMImage.NormalizedMaxError"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$img1 )</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Image Comparison"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"not equal"</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMimage.Release"; $img1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMimage.Release"; $img2)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.Composite
functions.description: Compose an image onto another at specified offset and using specified algorithm.

parameters.name: OtherImageRef
parameters.description: The second image reference (a number returned from the GMImage.New functions)

parameters.name: GravityType
parameters.description: The Gravity Type. Can be ForgetGravity=0, NorthWestGravity=1, NorthGravity=2, NorthEastGravity=3, WestGravity=4, CenterGravity=5, EastGravity=6, SouthWestGravity=7, SouthGravity=8, SouthEastGravity=9, StaticGravity=10.

parameters.name: CompositeOperator
parameters.description: Optional operator. Can be one of the following numbers: UndefinedCompositeOp = 0, OverCompositeOp = 1, InCompositeOp = 2, OutCompositeOp = 3, AtopCompositeOp = 4, XorCompositeOp = 5, PlusCompositeOp = 6, MinusCompositeOp = 7, AddCompositeOp = 8, SubtractCompositeOp = 9, DifferenceCompositeOp = 10, MultiplyCompositeOp = 11, BumpmapCompositeOp = 12, CopyCompositeOp = 13, CopyRedCompositeOp = 14, CopyGreenCompositeOp = 15, CopyBlueCompositeOp = 16, CopyOpacityCompositeOp = 17, ClearCompositeOp = 18, DissolveCompositeOp = 19, DisplaceCompositeOp = 20, ModulateCompositeOp = 21, ThresholdCompositeOp = 22, NoCompositeOp = 23, DarkenCompositeOp = 24, LightenCompositeOp = 25, HueCompositeOp = 26, SaturateCompositeOp = 27, ColorizeCompositeOp = 28, LuminizeCompositeOp = 29, CopyCyanCompositeOp = 32, CopyMagentaCompositeOp = 33, CopyYellowCompositeOp = 34, CopyBlackCompositeOp = 35, DivideCompositeOp = 36. Default is 2.

examples.label: Load image and compose it on another one:
examples.text_code:
# load images...
Set Variable [
$Image1
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.NewFromFile
"; "/Users/cs/Pictures/logo.png") ]
Set Variable [
$Image2
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.NewFromFile
"; "/Users/cs/Pictures/input.jpg") ]
# maybe scale it?
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Scale
";
$Image1
; "500x500") ]
# put first on second
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.Composite";
$Image2
;
$Image1
; 5; 1) ]
# and save to file
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToFile
";
$Image2
; "/Users/cs/Pictures/output.jpg") ]
# free memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Release
";
$Image1
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Release
";
$Image2
) ]

examples.label: Compose transparent picture on top of white background:
examples.text_code:
# Load from container
Set Variable [
$BigImageRef
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Test::BigImage
) ]
# make new picture
Set Variable [
$NewImageRef
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.New
";
MBS
("
GMImage.GetWidth
";
$BigImageRef
) & "x" &
MBS
("
GMImage.GetHeight
";
$BigImageRef
); "white") ]
# and compose
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.Composite";
$NewImageRef
;
$BigImageRef
; 5 /* center */; 1 /* over */) ]
# Store output and release the image
Set Field [
Test::Result
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer
";
$NewImageRef
) ]
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Free
";
$BigImageRef
) ]
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Free
";
$NewImageRef
) ]

functions.name: GMImage.CompositeXY
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.CompositeXY"; ImageRef; OtherImageRef; xOffset; yOffset { ; CompositeOperator } )
functions.description: Compose an image onto another at specified offset and using specified algorithm. You can use this function for example to add a watermark image.

parameters.name: OtherImageRef
parameters.description: The second image reference (a number returned from the GMImage.New functions)

parameters.name: CompositeOperator
parameters.description: Optional operator. Can be one of the following numbers: UndefinedCompositeOp = 0, OverCompositeOp = 1, InCompositeOp = 2, OutCompositeOp = 3, AtopCompositeOp = 4, XorCompositeOp = 5, PlusCompositeOp = 6, MinusCompositeOp = 7, AddCompositeOp = 8, SubtractCompositeOp = 9, DifferenceCompositeOp = 10, MultiplyCompositeOp = 11, BumpmapCompositeOp = 12, CopyCompositeOp = 13, CopyRedCompositeOp = 14, CopyGreenCompositeOp = 15, CopyBlueCompositeOp = 16, CopyOpacityCompositeOp = 17, ClearCompositeOp = 18, DissolveCompositeOp = 19, DisplaceCompositeOp = 20, ModulateCompositeOp = 21, ThresholdCompositeOp = 22, NoCompositeOp = 23, DarkenCompositeOp = 24, LightenCompositeOp = 25, HueCompositeOp = 26, SaturateCompositeOp = 27, ColorizeCompositeOp = 28, LuminizeCompositeOp = 29, CopyCyanCompositeOp = 32, CopyMagentaCompositeOp = 33, CopyYellowCompositeOp = 34, CopyBlackCompositeOp = 35, DivideCompositeOp = 36. Default is 2.

examples.label: Compose a picture on second:
examples.text_code:
# load images...
Set Variable [
$Image1
; Value:
MBS
( "

functions.name: GMImage.Contrast
functions.description: Contrast image (enhance intensity differences in image). Enhances the intensity differences between the lighter and darker elements of the image. Set sharpen to a value other than 0 to increase the image contrast otherwise the contrast is reduced.

parameters.name: sharpen
parameters.description: The sharpen value. An integer number, either 0 or 1.

examples.label: Changes contrast:
examples.text_code:
# load images...
Set Variable [
$Image
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
test::Input
) ]
# change constrast
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.Contrast";
$Image
; 1) ]
# and save to container
Set Field [
test::Output
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
";
$Image
; "test.png") ]
# free memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Release
";
$Image
) ]
examples.xml_code:

functions.name: GMImage.Copy
functions.description: Creates a copy of the picture. Please call GMImage.Release later to free memory for this image.
functions.result: Returns the ImageRef number on success.

functions.name: GMImage.CopyChannel
functions.description: Copies one channel from one image into channel of other image. e.g. to copy opacity from one image to other.

parameters.name: SourceImage
parameters.description: The image reference number of source image.

parameters.name: SourceChannel
parameters.description: The source channel. Can be Red, Green, Blue or Opacity.

parameters.name: DestImage
parameters.description: The image reference number of dest image.

parameters.name: DestChannel
parameters.description: The dest channel. Can be Red, Green, Blue or Opacity.

examples.label: Clip an image:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$gray
; Value: "#777777" ]
# Read in source image
Set Variable [
$SourceImage
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Clip Image::Input
) ]
Set Variable [
$sourceWidth
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.GetWidth
";
$SourceImage
) ]
Set Variable [
$sourceHeight
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.GetHeight
";
$SourceImage
) ]
#
# Scale to dest size
Set Variable [
$sourceSize
; Value: If(
$sourceWidth
>
$sourceHeight
;
$sourceHeight
;
$sourceWidth
) ]
Set Variable [
$destWidth
; Value: GetAsNumber (
Clip Image::Width
) ]
Set Variable [
$destHeight
; Value: GetAsNumber (
Clip Image::Height
) ]
Set Variable [
$destSize
; Value: If(
$destWidth
>
$destHeight
;
$destHeight
;
$destWidth
) ]
Set Variable [
$tempImage
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.New
";
$sourceSize
& "x" &
$sourceSize
; "transparent" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.CompositeXY
";
$tempImage
;
$sourceImage
; -(
$sourceWidth
-
$sourceSize
)/2; -(
$sourceHeight
-
$sourceSize
)/2; 1) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Scale
";
$tempImage
;
$destSize
& "x" &
$destSize
) ]
#
# Build mask
Set Variable [
$clipImage
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.New
";
$destSize
& "x" &
$destSize
; "transparent" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.SetFillColor
";
$clipImage
; "black" ) ]
Set Variable [
$radius
; Value:
$destSize
/2 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.DrawCircle
";
$clipImage
;
$radius
;
$radius
; 0;
$radius
) ]
#
# Apply mask
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.CopyChannel";
$tempImage
; "Opacity";
$clipImage
; "Opacity") ]
#
# Create destination image
Set Variable [
$destImage
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.New
";
$destWidth
& "x" &
$destHeight
;
$gray
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.CompositeXY
";
$destImage
;
$tempImage
; (
$destWidth
-
$destSize
)/2; (
$destHeight
-
$destSize
)/2; 1) ]
Set Field [
Clip Image::Output
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
";
$destImage
) ]
#
# Memory cleanup
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Release
";
$SourceImage
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Release
";
$tempImage
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Release
";
$clipImage
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"#777777" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$gray</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Read in source image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.NewFromContainer"; Clip Image::Input ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$SourceImage</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.GetWidth"; $SourceImage) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sourceWidth</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.GetHeight"; $SourceImage) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sourceHeight</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Scale to dest size</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If($sourceWidth > $sourceHeight; $sourceHeight; $sourceWidth) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sourceSize</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetAsNumber ( Clip Image::Width ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$destWidth</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetAsNumber ( Clip Image::Height ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$destHeight</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If($destWidth > $destHeight; $destHeight; $destWidth) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$destSize</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.New"; $sourceSize & "x" & $sourceSize; "transparent" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$tempImage</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.CompositeXY"; $tempImage; $sourceImage; -($sourceWidth-$sourceSize)/2; -($sourceHeight-$sourceSize)/2; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.Scale"; $tempImage; $destSize & "x" & $destSize ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Build mask</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.New"; $destSize & "x" & $destSize; "transparent" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$clipImage</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetFillColor"; $clipImage; "black" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$destSize/2 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$radius</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.DrawCircle"; $clipImage; $radius; $radius; 0; $radius ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Apply mask</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.CopyChannel"; $tempImage; "Opacity"; $clipImage; "Opacity") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Create destination image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.New"; $destWidth & "x" & $destHeight; $gray ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$destImage</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.CompositeXY"; $destImage; $tempImage; ($destWidth-$destSize)/2; ($destHeight-$destSize)/2; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $destImage) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Clip Image" id="99999" name="Output "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

functions.name: GMImage.Crop
functions.description: Crop image (subregion of original image). You can add pixels with GMImage.Border function.

parameters.name: Geometry
parameters.description: The rectangle to crop. Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200". With an optional offset "100x200+10+20". For percent values, add a percent on the end, for % values add a %: "100x200%!"

examples.label: Crops the image:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 
$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$Width = MBS("GMImage.GetWidth";$Image); 
$Height = MBS("GMImage.GetHeight";$Image); 
$Width = $Width/2;
$Height = $Height/2;
$ScaleResult = MBS("GMImage.Crop";$Image; $Width & "x" & $Height); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $Image); 
$Error = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 
$Result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Create barcode and crop it:
examples.text_code:
Set Field [
Barcode Generation::Image
; "" ]
Set Variable [
$img
; Value:
MBS
("
Barcode.Generate
";
Barcode Generation::Symbology
;
Barcode Generation::Text
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Set Variable [
$ImageHeight
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.GetHeight
";
$img
) ]
Set Variable [
$ImageWidth
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.GetWidth
";
$img
) ]
# calculate the crop rectangle
Set Variable [
$w
; Value:
$ImageWidth
]
Set Variable [
$h
; Value:
$ImageHeight
]
Set Variable [
$x
; Value: 0 ]
Set Variable [
$y
; Value:
$ImageHeight
- 50 ]
# crop
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("GMImage.Crop";
$img
;
$w
& "x" &
$h
& "+" &
$x
& "+" &
$y
) ]
Set Field [
Barcode Generation::Image
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
";
$img
; "barcode.png") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Release
";
$img
) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"" </Calculation>
    <Field table="Barcode Generation" id="99999" name="Image "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Barcode.Generate"; Barcode Generation::Symbology; Barcode Generation::Text) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$img</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.GetHeight"; $img) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ImageHeight</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.GetWidth"; $img) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ImageWidth</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> calculate the crop rectangle</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$ImageWidth </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$w</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$ImageHeight </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$h</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$x</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$ImageHeight - 50 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$y</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> crop</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Crop"; $img; $w & "x" & $h & "+" & $x & "+" & $y) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $img; "barcode.png") </Calculation>
    <Field table="Barcode Generation" id="99999" name="Image "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.Destroy"; $img ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

;

;
$Size

-

-

functions.name: GMImage.Describe
functions.description: Describes an image by printing its attributes. Attributes include the image width, height, size, and others.

parameters.name: verbose
parameters.description: Whether output should be verbose. Default is 1. Pass 0 to get a shorted output. Pass 2 to count the number of colors in the image.

functions.name: GMImage.DrawArc
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.DrawArc"; ImageRef; StartX; StartY; endX; endY; startDegrees; endDegrees )
functions.description: Draws an arc. Draw an arc using the stroke color and based on the circle starting at coordinates startX,startY, and ending with coordinates endX,endY, and bounded by the rotational arc startDegrees,endDegrees. If you have an open path, the command is added there for later drawing with GMImage.DrawPath .

parameters.name: StartX
parameters.description: The X coordinate for start position.

parameters.name: StartY
parameters.description: The Y coordinate for start position.

parameters.name: endX
parameters.description: The X coordinate for end position.

parameters.name: endY
parameters.description: The Y coordinate for end position.

functions.name: GMImage.DrawCircle
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.DrawCircle"; ImageRef; originX; originY; perimX; perimY )
functions.description: Draws a circle. Draw a circle using the stroke color and thickness using specified origin and perimeter coordinates. If a fill color is specified, then the object is filled. PerimX/PerimY define a point where the circle goes through, so put it a radius away from the origin. e.g. PerimX is originX + radius while PerimY is originY. If you have an open path, the command is added there for later drawing with GMImage.DrawPath .

parameters.name: originX
parameters.description: The X coordinate for start position.

parameters.name: originY
parameters.description: The Y coordinate for start position.

parameters.name: perimX
parameters.description: The X position of a point on the circle.

parameters.name: perimY
parameters.description: The Y position of a point on the circle.

examples.label: Draw bar charts:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>Let ( [  cities = List ( "Hamburg" ; "Berlin" ; "Munich" ; "Cologne" ) ;  values = List ( 1800000 ; 3700000 ; 1500000 ; 1100000 ) ;  colors = List ( "#4e79a7" ; "#f28e2b" ; "#e15759" ; "#76b7b2" ; "#59a14f" ) ;  img = MBS( "GMImage.New"; "600x400"; "white" ) ;  maxValue = Max ( 1800000 ; 3700000 ; 1500000 ; 1100000 ) ;  r = MBS( "GMImage.SetStrokeColor"; img; "black" ) ;  r = MBS( "GMImage.DrawLine"; img; 50; 350; 550; 350 ) ; // X-axis  r = MBS( "GMImage.DrawLine"; img; 50; 50; 50; 350 ) ;   // Y-axis  barWidth = 80 ;  spacing = 30 ;  baseX = 70 ;  r = Let ( [      count = ValueCount ( cities ) ;      colorCount = ValueCount ( colors ) ;      r = While (          [ i = 1 ] ; i ≤ count ;          [            city = GetValue ( cities ; i ) ;            val = GetValue ( values ; i ) ;            color = GetValue ( colors ; Mod ( i - 1 ; colorCount ) + 1 ) ;            height = (val / maxValue) * 300 ;            x1 = baseX + (i - 1) * (barWidth + spacing) ;            x2 = x1 + barWidth ;            y1 = 350 - height ;            y2 = 350 ;            r = MBS( "GMImage.SetFillColor"; img; color ) ;            r = MBS( "GMImage.DrawRectangle"; img; x1; y1; x2; y2 ) ;            r = MBS( "GMImage.SetFontPointsize"; img; 16 );                        geometry = barWidth & "x20+" & x1 & "+360" ;            r = MBS( "GMImage.Annotate"; img; city; geometry; 5 ) ; // 5 = CenterGravity            i = i + 1          ] ; r       )    ] ; r ) ;  result = MBS( "GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; img; "test.png" );  r = MBS("GMImage.Free"; img)  ] ; result ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="MyChart" id="99999" name="Chart "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.DrawRoundRectangle
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.DrawRoundRectangle"; ImageRef; upperLeftX; upperLeftY; lowerRightX; lowerRightY { ; cornerWidth; cornerHeight } )
functions.description: Draw a rounded rectangle. Draw a rounded rectangle using stroke color and thickness, with specified top left and right bottom coordinates, and specified corner width and height. If a fill color is specified, then the object is filled. You can set fill color to transparent to not draw it. If you have an open path, the command is added there for later drawing with GMImage.DrawPath .

parameters.name: cornerWidth
parameters.description: The width of the corner arc.

parameters.name: cornerHeight
parameters.description: The height of the corner arc.

functions.name: GMImage.Emboss
functions.description: Emboss image (hilight edges with 3D effect).

parameters.name: Radius
parameters.description: Optional, The radius of the Gaussian, in pixels, not counting the center pixel.

parameters.name: sigma
parameters.description: Optional, The standard deviation of the Laplacian, in pixels.

$RotateResult = MBS("GMImage.Emboss"; $Image; 10; 5);

functions.name: GMImage.ExifThumbnail
functions.description: Extracts an embedded thumbnail in EXIF data. Returns a JPEG in a container.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: File name to pass to FileMaker with container.

functions.name: GMImage.Extent
functions.description: Extends image size. Create an image canvas using background color sized according to geometry and composite existing image on it, with image placement controlled by gravity. Parameters are obtained from existing image properties if they are not specified via a method parameter.  Parameters which are supported by image properties (gravity and backgroundColor) update those image properties as a side-effect.

parameters.name: Geometry
parameters.description: The rectangle to use. Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200". With an optional offset "100x200+10+20". For percent values, add a percent on the end, for % values add a %: "100x200%!"

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: The background color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

parameters.name: GravityType
parameters.description: The Gravity Type. Can be number of text value. Can be ForgetGravity=0, NorthWestGravity=1, NorthGravity=2, NorthEastGravity=3, WestGravity=4, CenterGravity=5, EastGravity=6, SouthWestGravity=7, SouthGravity=8, SouthEastGravity=9, StaticGravity=10.

functions.name: GMImage.FindColorInColumn
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.FindColorInColumn"; ImageRef; X; Y; Color { ; Invert; Tolerance } )
functions.description: Locates first pixel in the column with given color. You can find first pixel matching your given color in the column starting with given y position and given tolerance. If you set Invert to 1, the plugin returns row for first pixel not matching color. Returns -1 if not found.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: The x coordinate. Zero based from 0 to GMImage.GetWidth -1.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: The y coordinate. Zero based from 0 to GMImage.GetHeight -1.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

parameters.name: Invert
parameters.description: If 0, we find first pixel matching your color. If 1 we find first pixel not matching the color. Default is 0.

parameters.name: Tolerance
parameters.description: The tolerance for comparison. Normally a small value like 3. So if color is 255, 255, 255 and pixel is 252, 252, 252 we have a match for tolerance being 3.

examples.label: Finds color in column:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.FindColorInColumn"; $img; $x; $y; $ColorToSearch; 1; 3)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.FindColorInRow
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.FindColorInRow"; ImageRef; X; Y; Color { ; Invert; Tolerance } )
functions.description: Locates first pixel in the row with given color. You can find first pixel matching your given color in the row starting with given x position and given tolerance. If you set Invert to 1, the plugin returns column for first pixel not matching color. Returns -1 if not found.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: The x coordinate. Zero based from 0 to GMImage.GetWidth -1.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: The y coordinate. Zero based from 0 to GMImage.GetHeight -1.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

parameters.name: Invert
parameters.description: If 0, we find first pixel matching your color. If 1 we find first pixel not matching the color. Default is 0.

parameters.name: Tolerance
parameters.description: The tolerance for comparison. Normally a small value like 3. So if color is 65535, 65535, 65535 and pixel is 64764, 64764, 64764 we have a match for tolerance being 800.

examples.label: Finds color in row:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.FindColorInRow"; $img; $x; $y; $ColorToSearch; 1; 3)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.Flatten
functions.description: Flatten the alpha channel. Please set background color to define what color goes into the transparent areas.

examples.label: Removes alpha channel:
examples.text_code:
# Load from container
Set Variable [
$BigImageRef
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Test::BigImage
) ]
# set the color to use for transparent areas
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetBackgroundColor
";
$BigImageRef
; "white") ]
# and remove transparency
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("GMImage.Flatten";
$BigImageRef
) ]
# Store output and release the image
Set Field [
Test::Result
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer
";
$BigImageRef
) ]
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Free
";
$BigImageRef
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; Test::BigImage) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$BigImageRef</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set the color to use for transparent areas</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetBackgroundColor"; $BigImageRef; "white") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and remove transparency</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Flatten"; $BigImageRef) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Store output and release the image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer"; $BigImageRef ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Test" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Free";$BigImageRef) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Error</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.Flip
functions.description: Flip image (reflect each scanline in the vertical direction)

examples.label: Mirror the picture.
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( 
[ 
$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$RotateResult = MBS("GMImage.Flip"; $Image); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $Image); 
$DestroyResult = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 
$Result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.FloodFillColor
functions.description: Flood-fill color across pixels. Fill pixels starting at target-pixel and stopping at pixels matching specified border color. Uses current fuzz setting when determining color match:

parameters.name: Geometry
parameters.description: The point. A Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200". With an optional offset "100x200+10+20". For percent values, add a percent on the end, for % values add a %: "100x200%!"

parameters.name: FillColor
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

parameters.name: borderColor
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

functions.name: GMImage.FloodFillColorXY
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.FloodFillColorXY"; ImageRef; X; Y; FillColor { ; borderColor } )
functions.description: Flood-fill color across pixels starting at target-pixel and stopping at pixels matching specified border color. Uses current fuzz setting when determining color match. See GMImage.SetColorFuzz function.

parameters.name: FillColor
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

parameters.name: borderColor
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

examples.label: Make a picture transparent:
examples.text_code:
#load a picture
Set Variable [
$image
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Test::BigImage
)]
#white in RGB
Set Variable [
$f
; Value:"white"]
#Replace all white with nearly white
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.ReplaceColor
";
$image
;
$f
; "COLOR 65535 65278 65535"; 0)]
#now flood fill it with 5 as tolerance
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.SetColorFuzz
";
$image
; 5 )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.FloodFillColorXY";
$image
; 0; 0;
$f
)]
#now set back fuzz and make transparent using white
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.SetColorFuzz
";
$image
; 0 )]
Set Variable [
$imagecolor
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Transparent
";
$image
;
$f
)]
#write back to container
Set Field [
Test::Result
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
";
$image
;)]
#cleanup
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$image
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>load a picture</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.NewFromContainer"; Test::BigImage)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>white in RGB</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"white"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$f</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Replace all white with nearly white</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.ReplaceColor"; $image; $f; "COLOR 65535 65278 65535"; 0)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>now flood fill it with 5 as tolerance</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetColorFuzz"; $image; 5 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.FloodFillColorXY"; $image; 0; 0; $f )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>now set back fuzz and make transparent using white</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetColorFuzz"; $image; 0 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.Transparent"; $image; $f)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$imagecolor</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>write back to container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $image;)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Test" id="99999" name="Result"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMimage.Release";$image)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Error</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Replace the surrounding color of an image with transparency:
examples.text_code:
# load an image
Set Variable [
$image
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Drawing::Image
) ]
# now read a pixel from top left near the edfge
Set Variable [
$c
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.GetPixelColor
";
$image
; 1; 1 ) ]
# we want to be tolerant. Color range from 0 to 65535, so 257 * 50 may be a good value
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.SetColorFuzz
";
$image
; 257 *
Drawing::gTolerance
) ]
# fill the area with the color to make it all one color (and remove JPEG artefacts)
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.FloodFillColorXY";
$image
; 1; 1;
$c
) ]
# now swap with transparency
Set Variable [
$imagecolor
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Transparent
";
$image
;
$c
; "transparent") ]
# write back to field
Set Field [
Drawing::gArtTemp
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
";
$image
) ]
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$image
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> load an image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.NewFromContainer"; Drawing::Image) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now read a pixel from top left near the edfge</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.GetpixelColor"; $image; 1; 1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$c</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> we want to be tolerant. Color range from 0 to 65535, so 257 * 50 may be a good value</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetColorFuzz"; $image; 257 * Drawing::gTolerance ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> fill the area with the color to make it all one color (and remove JPEG artefacts)</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.FloodFillColorXY"; $image; 1; 1; $c ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now swap with transparency</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.Transparent"; $image; $c; "transparent") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$imagecolor</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> write back to field</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $image) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Drawing" id="99999" name="gArtTemp "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Destroy";$image) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Error</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.FloodFillOpacity
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.FloodFillOpacity"; ImageRef; X; Y; Opacity; PaintMethod )
functions.description: Floodfill pixels matching color (within fuzz factor) of target pixel(x,y) with replacement opacity value using method.

functions.name: GMImage.FloodFillTexture
functions.description: Flood-fill texture across pixels. Fills pixels starting at target-pixel and stopping at pixels matching specified border color. Uses current fuzz setting when determining color match.

parameters.name: Geometry
parameters.description: The point. A Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200". With an optional offset "100x200+10+20". For percent values, add a percent on the end, for % values add a %: "100x200%!"

parameters.name: borderColor
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

functions.name: GMImage.FloodFillTextureXY
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.FloodFillTextureXY"; ImageRef; X; Y; texture { ; borderColor } )
functions.description: Flood-fill texture across pixels. Fills pixels that match the color of the target pixel and are neighbors of the target pixel. Uses current fuzz setting when determining color match.

parameters.name: borderColor
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

functions.name: GMImage.FontMap
functions.description: Queries current font map in use. The MBS Plugin provides to GraphicsMagick the font map to use. This is a XML defining which fonts are available. Use this function to learn what fonts may be available or debug to see why a font doesn't load.

functions.name: GMImage.FontTypeMetrics
functions.description: Queries space needed for text. Obtain font metrics for text string given current font, point size, and density settings. Returns list with following values: text width in pixel text height in pixel Ascent, the distance in pixels from the text baseline to the highest/upper grid coordinate used to place an outline point. Descent, the distance in pixels from the baseline to the lowest grid coordinate used to place an outline point. Always a negative value. Maximum horizontal advance in pixels. Font size To extract values, please pass through Math.TextToNumber or similar functions which always uses dot as decimal separator. See also Font.CalculateTextWidth and DynaPDF.GetFTextHeight functions.

examples.label: Draw some text on a picture:
examples.text_code:
# open a picture
Set Variable [
$ref
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Annotate Image::InputImage
) ]
# set font size
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetFontPointSize
";
$ref
; 20) ]
# set the font
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetFont
";
$ref
; "@/System/Library/Fonts/LucidaGrande.ttc") ]
# set the fill color
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetFillColor
";
$ref
; "RGB 0 0 0") ]
# Query size for this text:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("GMImage.FontTypeMetrics";
$ref
;
Annotate Image::Text
) ]
# draw the text:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Annotate
";
$ref
;
Annotate Image::Text
; "100x200+10+40"; 1) ]
# write image
Set Field [
Annotate Image::OutputImage
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
";
$ref
) ]
# free memory
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$ref
) ]

functions.name: GMImage.Format
functions.description: Long image format description. e.g. "Joint Photographic Experts Group JFIF format" for JPEG "Portable Network Graphics" for PNG "Tagged Image File Format" for TIFF "Microsoft Windows bitmap image" for BMP "CompuServe graphics interchange format" for GIF.
functions.result: Returns the long image format description.
