functions.name: GMImage.Frame
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.Frame"; ImageRef; Width; Height; innerBevel; outerBevel )
functions.description: Frame image.

examples.label: Adds a frame to the image:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( 
[ 
$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$RotateResult = MBS("GMImage.Frame"; $Image); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $Image); 
$DestroyResult = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 
$Result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.Free
functions.description: Releases the image from memory. Please call this function when you are done with the image to free memory. The plugin doesn't know whether you need the image later, so it can't free the memory for you. You can use GMImage.ImageCount to find out how many images are open in memory.

functions.name: GMImage.FreeAll
functions.description: Destroys all images.

functions.name: GMImage.Gamma
functions.description: Gamma correct image. You can also pass only one gamma value for all three channels.

parameters.name: gammaRed
parameters.description: The gamma value for the red channel. (or if only one value is given the gamma value for all channels)

parameters.name: gammaGreen
parameters.description: The gamma value for the green channel.

parameters.name: gammaBlue
parameters.description: The gamma value for the blue channel.

functions.name: GMImage.GaussianBlur
functions.description: Gaussian blur image. This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 GMImage.Gamma - GMImage.GaussianBlurChannel 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The number of neighbor pixels to be included in the convolution mask is specified by width

parameters.name: sigma
parameters.description: Specifies the standard deviation of the gaussian bell curve

functions.name: GMImage.GaussianBlurChannel
functions.description: Gaussian blur image. This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 GMImage.GaussianBlur - GMImage.Geometry 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: ChannelType
parameters.description: One of the following values: RedChannel = 1 (RGB Red channel), CyanChannel = 2 (CMYK Cyan channel), GreenChannel = 3 (RGB Green channel), MagentaChannel = 4 (CMYK Magenta channel), BlueChannel = 5 (RGB Blue channel), YellowChannel = 6 (CMYK Yellow channel), OpacityChannel = 7 (Opacity channel), BlackChannel = 8 (CMYK Black (K) channel), MatteChannel = 9 (Same as Opacity channel (deprecated)), AllChannels = 10 (Color channels), GrayChannel = 11 (Color channels represent an intensity.)

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The number of neighbor pixels to be included in the convolution mask is specified by width

parameters.name: sigma
parameters.description: Specifies the standard deviation of the gaussian bell curve

functions.name: GMImage.Geometry
functions.description: Preferred size of the image when encoding. This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 GMImage.GaussianBlurChannel - GMImage.GetAdjoin 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: GMImage.GetAdjoin
functions.description: Get adjoin value. Join images into a single multi-image file

functions.name: GMImage.GetAnimationDelay
functions.description: Queries the animation delay. Time in 1/100ths of a second which must expire before displaying the next image in an animated sequence. Time in 1/100ths of a second (0 to 65535) which must expire before displaying the next image in an animated sequence. This option is useful for regulating the animation of a sequence of GIF images within Netscape.
functions.result: Returns animation delay value as number.

functions.name: GMImage.GetAnimationIterations
functions.description: Queries the Number of iterations to loop an animation (e.g. Netscape loop extension) for.
functions.result: Returns the Number of iterations to loop an animation for.

functions.name: GMImage.GetAntiAlias
functions.description: Returns whether to Anti-alias Postscript and TrueType fonts (default true)

functions.name: GMImage.GetAttribute
functions.description: Query a named attribute. Any number of named attributes may be attached to the image. For example, the image comment is a named image attribute with the name "comment". EXIF tags are attached to the image as named attributes. Use the syntax "EXIF:<tag>" to request an EXIF tag similar to "EXIF:DateTime": Unicode characters are escaped with backslash followed by three digits. If encoding is UTF-8 for example the encoding \303\244 represents an "ä". This changed in v15.3 where the plugin automatically decodes this. For IPTC keywords, better use GMImage.GetIPTCProfileValues to get all keywords.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding. Default is UTF-8. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.name: GMImage.GetColorSpace
functions.description: Queries the Image Color Space UndefinedColorspace = 0, RGBColorspace = 1 (Plain old RGB colorspace), GRAYColorspace = 2 (Plain old full-range grayscale), TransparentColorspace = 3 (RGB but preserve matte channel during quantize), OHTAColorspace = 4, XYZColorspace = 5 (CIE XYZ), YCCColorspace = 6 (Kodak PhotoCD PhotoYCC), YIQColorspace = 7, YPbPrColorspace = 8, YUVColorspace = 9, CMYKColorspace = 10 (Cyan, magenta, yellow, black, alpha), sRGBColorspace = 11 (Kodak PhotoCD sRGB), HSLColorspace = 12 (Hue, saturation, luminosity), HWBColorspace = 13 (Hue, whiteness, blackness), LABColorspace = 14 (LAB colorspace not supported yet other than via lcms), CineonLogRGBColorspace = 15 (RGB data with Cineon Log scaling, 2.048 density range), Rec601LumaColorspace = 16 (Luma (Y) according to ITU-R 601), Rec601YCbCrColorspace = 17 (YCbCr according to ITU-R 601), Rec709LumaColorspace = 18 (Luma (Y) according to ITU-R 709), Rec709YCbCrColorspace = 19 (YCbCr according to ITU-R 709)
functions.result: Returns the color space type as number.

; Value:

"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/image2.png") ]

("GMImage.GetColorSpace";
$image
) ]

$value
]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$image
) ]

    </Value>
    <Repetition>

      </Button>

  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.GetDebug
functions.description: Queries whether printing of debug messages from ImageMagick is enabled.

functions.name: GMImage.GetDefineSet
functions.description: Queries a define value. Tagged image format define. Similar to the get/setdefineValue() functions except that passing the flag value 'true' creates a value-less define with that format and key. Passing the flag value 'false' removes any existing matching definition. The method returns 'true' if a matching key exists, and 'false' if no matching key exists.

functions.name: GMImage.GetDefineValue
functions.description: Queries a value. Tagged image format define (set/access coder-specific option) The magick option specifies the coder the define applies to.  The key option provides the key specific to that coder.  The value option provides the value to set (if any). See the get/set defineSet functions if the key must be removed entirely.

functions.name: GMImage.GetFileName
functions.description: Queries the Image file name.

functions.name: GMImage.GetFileSize
functions.description: Returns the number of bytes of the image on disk

functions.name: GMImage.GetFillColor
functions.description: Queries the Color to use when filling drawn objects.
functions.result: Returns a color value as string.

functions.name: GMImage.GetFillRule
functions.description: Queries the rule to use when filling drawn objects UndefinedRule = 0, EvenOddRule = 1, NonZeroRule = 2

functions.name: GMImage.GetFilterType
functions.description: Queries the Filter to use when resizing image UndefinedFilter = 0, PointFilter = 1, BoxFilter = 2, TriangleFilter = 3, HermiteFilter = 4, HanningFilter = 5, HammingFilter = 6, BlackmanFilter = 7, GaussianFilter = 8, QuadraticFilter = 9, CubicFilter = 10, CatromFilter = 11, MitchellFilter = 12, LanczosFilter = 13, BesselFilter = 14, SincFilter = 15

functions.name: GMImage.GetFont
functions.description: Queries the Text rendering font.

functions.name: GMImage.GetFontFamily
functions.description: Queries font family.

functions.name: GMImage.GetFontPointSize
functions.description: Queries the Font point size.

functions.name: GMImage.GetFontStretch
functions.description: Queries font stretch. Can be Normal, UltraCondensed, ExtraCondensed, Condensed, SemiCondensed, SemiExpanded, Expanded, ExtraExpanded,  UltraExpanded or Any.

functions.name: GMImage.GetFontStyle
functions.description: Queries font style. Can be Normal, Italic, Oblique or Any.

functions.name: GMImage.GetFontWeight
functions.description: Queries font weight. Range about 0 to 1000. 400 is normal and 800 is bold.

functions.name: GMImage.GetGamma
functions.description: Gamma level of the image
functions.result: Returns the Gamma level of the image as number.

functions.name: GMImage.GetGifDisposeMethod
functions.description: Queries the GIF disposal method.

functions.name: GMImage.GetHeight
functions.description: Returns the image height.

examples.label: Show with and height
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 
$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$Width = MBS("GMImage.GetWidth";$Image); 
$Height = MBS("GMImage.GetHeight";$Image); 
$Error = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 
$Width & " x " & $Height )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.GetJPEGAsBase64

functions.name: GMImage.GetJPEGAsHex

    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>

    </Title>

      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.GetMagick

functions.name: GMImage.GetMatte

functions.name: GMImage.GetMatteColor

functions.name: GMImage.GetModulusDepth

functions.name: GMImage.GetMonochrome

functions.name: GMImage.GetPNGAsBase64
functions.description: Returns the picture in PNG format as a base64 encoded string. For the quality setting use the SetQuality function. Useful for sending PNG image as email.

functions.name: GMImage.GetPNGAsHex
functions.description: Returns the picture in PNG format as a hex encoded string. For the quality setting use the SetQuality function. Useful for storing in database.

functions.name: GMImage.GetPage
functions.description: Queries the Preferred size and location of an image canvas.

functions.name: GMImage.GetPixelColor
functions.description: Get pixel color at location x & y. Returned color is 16bit in v9.0 or later. You may need to divide values by 257 if you need 8 bit values.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: The horizontal position. Range from 0 to GMImage.GetWidth -1.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: The vertical position. Range from 0 to GMImage.GetHeight -1.

functions.result: Returns a color value as string.

examples.label: Open picture and read first pixel:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 

$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$colorValue = MBS("GMImage.GetPixelColor"; $Image; 0; 0); 
$Error = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 

$colorValue )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Trims space around image and make transparent:

Set Variable [

GMImage.SetBackgroundColor
";

; "RGB 1 0 0" )]

$Result

";
$BigImageRef
)]
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:

( "
GMImage.SetColorFuzz
";

$Result
; Value:

GMImage.SetMatte
";
$BigImageRef
; 1 )]

$farbe
; Value:

$BigImageRef
; 0; 0 )]
Set Variable [

GMImage.ReplaceColor

$BigImageRef
;
$farbe

examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$BigImageRef</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetBackgroundColor"; $BigImageRef; "RGB 1 0 0" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Trim"; $BigImageRef)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetColorFuzz"; $BigImageRef; 5 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetMatte"; $BigImageRef; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.GetPixelColor"; $BigImageRef; 0; 0 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$farbe</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMimage.Release";$BigImageRef)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Error</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.GetProfile
functions.description: Retrieve a named profile from the image. Valid names are: "8BIM", "8BIMTEXT", "APP1", "EXIF", "APP1JPEG", "ICC", "ICM", & "IPTC" or an existing user/format-defined profile name.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: the string with the name of the profile.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the profile. Default is "profile".

functions.result: Returns a container file value with the embedded profile file.

examples.label: Get EXIF data blob:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "GMImage.Getprofile";
$img
; "EXIF" )
examples.xml_code:

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.Getprofile"; $img; "EXIF" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.GetQuality
functions.description: Queries the JPEG/MIFF/PNG compression level (default 75).

functions.name: GMImage.GetQuantizeColorSpace
functions.description: Gets the Colorspace to quantize in. UndefinedColorspace = 0, RGBColorspace = 1 (Plain old RGB colorspace), GRAYColorspace = 2 (Plain old full-range grayscale), TransparentColorspace = 3 (RGB but preserve matte channel during quantize), OHTAColorspace = 4, XYZColorspace = 5 (CIE XYZ), YCCColorspace = 6 (Kodak PhotoCD PhotoYCC), YIQColorspace = 7, YPbPrColorspace = 8, YUVColorspace = 9, CMYKColorspace = 10 (Cyan, magenta, yellow, black, alpha), sRGBColorspace = 11 (Kodak PhotoCD sRGB), HSLColorspace = 12 (Hue, saturation, luminosity), HWBColorspace = 13 (Hue, whiteness, blackness), LABColorspace = 14 (LAB colorspace not supported yet other than via lcms), CineonLogRGBColorspace = 15 (RGB data with Cineon Log scaling, 2.048 density range), Rec601LumaColorspace = 16 (Luma (Y) according to ITU-R 601), Rec601YCbCrColorspace = 17 (YCbCr according to ITU-R 601), Rec709LumaColorspace = 18 (Luma (Y) according to ITU-R 709), Rec709YCbCrColorspace = 19 (YCbCr according to ITU-R 709)

functions.name: GMImage.GetQuantizeColors
functions.description: Queries the Maximum number of colors to quantize to.

functions.name: GMImage.GetQuantizeDither
functions.description: Queries whether to dither image during quantization (default true).

functions.name: GMImage.GetQuantizeTreeDepth
functions.description: Queries the Quantization tree-depth.

functions.name: GMImage.GetQuiet
functions.description: Queries quiet setting. If enabled, suppress all warning messages. Error messages are still reported.

functions.name: GMImage.GetRenderingIntent
functions.description: Queries the type of rendering intent. UndefinedIntent = 0, SaturationIntent = 1, PerceptualIntent = 2, AbsoluteIntent = 3, RelativeIntent = 4

functions.name: GMImage.GetResolutionUnits
functions.description: Queries which units are used for image resolution. UndefinedResolution = 0, PixelsPerInchResolution = 1, PixelsPerCentimeterResolution = 2

examples.label: Queries the resolution unit and fix it, if not set.
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$res
; Value:
MBS
("GMImage.GetResolutionUnits";
$BigImageRef
) ]
If [
$res
= 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetResolutionUnits
";
$BigImageRef
; 1) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.GetResolutionUnits"; $BigImageRef) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$res</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $res = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetResolutionUnits"; $BigImageRef; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.GetStrokeAntiAlias
functions.description: Whether stroke anti-aliasing is enabled.

functions.name: GMImage.GetStrokeColor
functions.description: Queries the Color to use when drawing object outlines.
functions.result: Returns a color value as string.

functions.name: GMImage.GetStrokeDashArray
functions.description: Queries the pattern of dashes and gaps used to stroke paths. The strokeDashArray represents an array of numbers that specify the lengths of alternating dashes and gaps in pixels. If an odd number of values is provided, then the list of values is repeated to yield an even number of values. A typical strokeDashArray array might contain the members 5 3 2.
functions.result: Returns list of values or error.

functions.name: GMImage.GetStrokeDashOffset
functions.description: Gets the stroke dash offset. While drawing using a dash pattern, specify distance into the dash pattern to start the dash (default 0).

functions.name: GMImage.GetStrokeLineCap
functions.description: Specify the shape to be used at the end of open subpaths when they are stroked. UndefinedCap = 0, ButtCap = 1, RoundCap = 2, SquareCap = 3

functions.name: GMImage.GetStrokeLineJoin
functions.description: Specify the shape to be used at the corners of paths (or other vector shapes) when they are stroked. UndefinedJoin = 0, MiterJoin = 1, RoundJoin = 2, BevelJoin = 3

functions.name: GMImage.GetStrokeMiterLimit
functions.description: Gets the miter limit. When two line segments meet at a sharp angle and miter joins have been specified for 'lineJoin', it is possible for the miter to extend far beyond the thickness of the line stroking the path. The miterLimit' imposes a limit on the ratio of the miter length to the 'lineWidth'. The default value of this parameter is 4.

functions.name: GMImage.GetStrokeWidth
functions.description: Gets the Stroke width for drawing vector objects (default one)

functions.name: GMImage.GetSubImage
functions.description: Queries which image of an image sequence is currently used.

functions.name: GMImage.GetSubRange
functions.description: Gets the Number of images relative to the base image

functions.name: GMImage.GetTextEncoding
functions.description: Queries the text encoding.

functions.name: GMImage.GetTileName
functions.description: Queries the Tile name.

functions.name: GMImage.GetType
functions.description: Image representation type. UndefinedType = 0, BilevelType = 1, GrayscaleType = 2, GrayscaleMatteType = 3, PaletteType = 4, PaletteMatteType = 5, TrueColorType = 6, TrueColorMatteType = 7, ColorSeparationType = 8, ColorSeparationMatteType = 9, OptimizeType = 10

functions.name: GMImage.GetZPL
functions.description: Queries image as ZPL hex image. This is for sending data to a receipt printer. The picture is read as black & white image and we build the hex string, you can send to the printer. You can pass in a color, grayscale or b&w image. Picture needs to have a width dividable by 8.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Pass various flags. Currently 1 for skipping header/footer.

examples.label: Get ZPL encoded image:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$BigImageRef
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Pictures::Image
) ]
# put the ZPL image in container:
Set Field [
Pictures::Result
;
MBS

functions.name: GMImage.Hash
functions.description: Calculates a hash of the image. Hash is returned as 64 characters being 1 or 0. We convert image to 8x8, turn grayscale and check if pixels are above or below mean value. This hash is quite immune against resizing, compression artifacts and hue changes. You can use Text.LevenshteinDistance or Text.JaroWinklerDistance to compare two hashes. While GMImage.Hash does make a finger print, you can use Hash.Digest to get a hash of the image bytes.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: The size of the bitmap. Value from 8 to 1024. Default is 8.

examples.label: Play with hash function:

$ImageRef
; Value:

GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Test::BigImage
) ]

$Hash1
; Value:

$ImageRef

$r
; Value:

GMImage.Flip
";
$ImageRef
) ]
Set Variable [
$Hash2
; Value:
MBS
("GMImage.Hash";
$ImageRef
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Flop
";
$ImageRef
) ]
Set Variable [
$Hash3
; Value:
MBS
("GMImage.Hash";
$ImageRef
) ]
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$ImageRef
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Hash original: " &
$hash1
& ¶ & "Hash after 1x mirror: " &
$hash2
& ¶ & "Hash after 2x mirror: " &… ]

functions.name: GMImage.ImageCount
functions.description: Queries number of open images. This function allows you to check if you properly released all images from memory when your script is done. See also GMImage.Release .

functions.name: GMImage.Implode
functions.description: Implode image (special effect).

examples.label: Applies the implode effect:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 
$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.Implode";$Image; 5); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $Image); 
$Error = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 
$Result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.InvertChannel
functions.description: Inverts an image. All pixel values are negated. This is same as MBS( " GMImage.MultiplyChannel "; ImageRef; Channel; -1; 0; 65535), but a bit faster.

parameters.name: Channel
parameters.description: Which channel to work with. Can be opacity, red, green or blue. The value rgb will apply effect to all three color channels.

$image
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Invert::Input
) ]

; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.InvertChannel";
$image
; "red") ]
// Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.InvertChannel";
$image
) ]
// Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.InvertChannel";
$image
; "blue") ]
# all channels...
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.InvertChannel";
$image
; "rgb") ]
Set Field [
Invert::Output
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
";
$image
; "output.png") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Release
";
$image
) ]

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="False" id="141" name="Set Variable">

      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.InvertChannel"; $image) </Calculation>
    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="False" id="141" name="Set Variable">

functions.name: GMImage.IsGray
functions.description: Checks whether all pixels are gray. Returns 1 for gray or 0 if one pixel at least is not gray.

functions.name: GMImage.IsLoggingEnabled
functions.description: Checks whether logging is enabled. Returns 1 if we log GraphicsMagick usage.

functions.name: GMImage.Level
functions.description: Level image. Adjust the levels of the image by scaling the colors falling between specified white and black points to the full available quantum range. The parameters provided represent the black, mid (gamma), and white points.  The black point specifies the darkest color in the image. Colors darker than the black point are set to zero. Mid point (gamma) specifies a gamma correction to apply to the image. White point specifies the lightest color in the image.  Colors brighter than the white point are set to the maximum quantum value. The black and white point have the valid range 0 to MaxRGB while mid (gamma) has a useful range of 0 to ten.

functions.name: GMImage.LevelChannel
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.LevelChannel"; ImageRef; ChannelType; blackPoint; whitePoint { ; midPoint } )
functions.description: Level image channel. Adjust the levels of the image by scaling the colors falling between specified white and black points to the full available quantum range. The parameters provided represent the black, mid (gamma), and white points.  The black point specifies the darkest color in the image. Colors darker than the black point are set to zero. Mid point (gamma) specifies a gamma correction to apply to the image. White point specifies the lightest color in the image.  Colors brighter than the white point are set to the maximum quantum value. The black and white point have the valid range 0 to MaxRGB while mid (gamma) has a useful range of 0 to ten.

parameters.name: ChannelType
parameters.description: One of the following values: RedChannel = 1 (RGB Red channel), CyanChannel = 2 (CMYK Cyan channel), GreenChannel = 3 (RGB Green channel), MagentaChannel = 4 (CMYK Magenta channel), BlueChannel = 5 (RGB Blue channel), YellowChannel = 6 (CMYK Yellow channel), OpacityChannel = 7 (Opacity channel), BlackChannel = 8 (CMYK Black (K) channel), MatteChannel = 9 (Same as Opacity channel (deprecated)), AllChannels = 10 (Color channels), GrayChannel = 11 (Color channels represent an intensity.)

functions.name: GMImage.List
functions.description: Lists all IDs of images in memory. Returns IDs for all images in memory. If this list is growing, you may have forgotten to call GMImage.Release in your scripts. Normally after all scripts are done, this list should be empty, except for static images you want to load once and reuse from time to time.

examples.label: List all object IDs:

$list
; Value:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.ListTypeInfo
functions.description: Writes type list file. To debug font issues, this function can write a list of fonts to a file. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

functions.name: GMImage.Map
functions.description: Remap image colors with closest color from reference image.

parameters.name: dither
parameters.description: Optional, a boolean whether to dither.

functions.name: GMImage.MatteFloodfill
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.MatteFloodfill"; ImageRef; Target; Opacity; X; Y; PaintMethod )
functions.description: Floodfill designated area with replacement opacity value. This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 11st November 2019 GMImage.Map - GMImage.MeanErrorPerPixel 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: Target
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

functions.name: GMImage.MeanErrorPerPixel
functions.description: The mean error per pixel computed when an image is color reduced. This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 GMImage.MatteFloodfill - GMImage.MedianFilter 💬 Ask a question or report a problem
functions.result: Returns the mean error per pixel as number.

functions.name: GMImage.MedianFilter
functions.description: Filter image by replacing each pixel component with the median color in a circular neighborhood. This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 GMImage.MeanErrorPerPixel - GMImage.Minify 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: GMImage.MontageGeometry
functions.description: Tile size and offset within an image montage. This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 GMImage.Modulate - GMImage.MotionBlur 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: GMImage.MotionBlur
functions.description: Motion blur image with specified blur factor. This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 GMImage.MontageGeometry - GMImage.MultiplyChannel 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: Radius
parameters.description: Specifies the radius of the Gaussian, in pixels, not counting the center pixel.

parameters.name: sigma
parameters.description: Specifies the standard deviation of the Laplacian, in pixels.

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: specifies the angle the object appears to be comming from (zero degrees is from the right).

functions.name: GMImage.MultiplyChannel
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.MultiplyChannel"; ImageRef; Channel; Factor { ; Add1; Add2 } )
functions.description: Multiplies all values in a channel. For every pixel component, we apply the formula: newValue = ((Add1 + oldValue) * Factor) + Add2 This can be used to change brightness or make opacity less/more transparent.

parameters.name: Channel
parameters.description: Which channel to work with. Can be opacity, red, green or blue. The value rgb will apply effect to all three color channels.

parameters.name: Factor
parameters.description: The multiplication factor. Range from -1.0 to 1.0.

parameters.name: Add1
parameters.description: The value to add before multiply. Usually between -65535 and 65535.

parameters.name: Add2
parameters.description: The value to add after multiply. Usually in range from -65535 to 65535.

examples.label: Multiplies existing opacity channel to make image 90% transparent:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMimage.MultiplyChannel"; $ImageRef;  "opacity"; 0.1; 0; 255*0.9)
// existing values * 0.1 and add 90% to the value to move to higher range.</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.Negate
functions.description: Negate colors in image.

parameters.name: grayscale
parameters.description: Set grayscale to only negate grayscale values in image.

examples.label: Negates an image:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 
$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.Negate";$Image); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $Image); 
$Error = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 
$Result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.New
functions.description: Construct a blank image canvas of specified size and color. Please call GMImage.Release later to free memory for this image. Image reference numbers are starting at 18000 and counting up for each new image.

parameters.name: Geometry
parameters.description: The rectangle. Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200". With an optional offset "100x200+10+20". For percent values, add a percent on the end, for % values add a %: "100x200%!"

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

functions.result: Returns the ImageRef number on success.

functions.name: GMImage.NewFromBase64
functions.description: Loads a picture from base64 encoded data. Please call GMImage.Release later to free memory for this image. This is useful for images you got from an email. Image reference numbers are starting at 18000 and counting up for each new image.
functions.result: Returns the ImageRef number on success.

functions.name: GMImage.NewFromContainer
functions.description: Loads a picture from the given container. Please call GMImage.Release later to free memory for this image. Image reference numbers are starting at 18000 and counting up for each new image. If image doesn't load, please check whether it's a HEIC, HEIF or RAW image. For those, please use Container.ReadImage first to convert on macOS/iOS/Windows. When the container has a PDF, the plugin may just load the preview image. Please use DynaPDF.RenderPage , PDFKit.GetPDFPageImage or WinPDF.PageImage to render an image from the PDF first, before loading it.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: A value from a Container Field. Can include various image formats like JPEG, PNG or just containers with FILE data.

functions.result: Returns the ImageRef number on success.

examples.label: Show with and height
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 
$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$Width = MBS("GMImage.GetWidth";$Image); 
$Height = MBS("GMImage.GetHeight";$Image); 
$Error = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 
$Width & " x " & $Height )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.NewFromFile
functions.description: Loads a picture file. Please call GMImage.Release later to free memory for this image. Image reference numbers are starting at 18000 and counting up for each new image. If image doesn't load, please check whether it's a HEIC, HEIF or RAW image. For those, please use Container.ReadImage first to convert on macOS/iOS/Windows to PNG and then use GMImage.NewFromContainer function. When the file is a PDF, please use DynaPDF.RenderPage , PDFKit.GetPDFPageImage or WinPDF.PageImage to render an image from the PDF first, before loading it. On Windows the file APIs in GraphicsMagick only do ANSI file paths. MacOS and Linux can do full Unicode. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Magick
parameters.description: The magick setting to define which codec to use.

functions.result: Returns the ImageRef number on success.

examples.label: Load an image from file path:
examples.text_code:
# Loads a picture file and returns the ImageRef number on success
Set Variable [
$RefNum
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.NewFromFile";
$currentPicturePath
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Loads a picture file and returns the ImageRef number on success</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.NewFromFile"; $currentPicturePath ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$RefNum</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.NewFromHex
functions.description: Loads a picture from hex encoded data. Please call GMImage.Release later to free memory for this image. This is useful for images you got from a database. Image reference numbers are starting at 18000 and counting up for each new image.
functions.result: Returns the ImageRef number on success.

functions.name: GMImage.NewImagesFromContainer
functions.description: Loads a picture from the given container. This is for loading multi image tiff files. Please call GMImage.Release later to free memory each image. Image reference numbers are starting at 18000 and counting up for each new image.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: A value from a Container Field.

functions.result: Returns list of image references or error.

functions.name: GMImage.NewImagesFromFile
functions.description: Loads a picture file with several files. This is for loading multi image tiff files. Please call GMImage.Release later to free memory for each image. Image reference numbers are starting at 18000 and counting up for each new image.
functions.result: Returns list of image references or error.

examples.label: Loads from file and writes to other file:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$img
; Value:
MBS
("GMImage.NewImagesFromFile"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.jpg")]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToFile
";
$img
; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.tif" )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Release
";
$img
)]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.NewImagesFromFile"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.jpg")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$img</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("IsError") = 0</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.WriteToFile"; $img; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.tif" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

    </Repetition>

  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>

functions.name: GMImage.NormalizedMeanError
functions.description: The normalized mean error per pixel computed when an image is color reduced.
functions.result: Returns the normalized mean error as number.

functions.name: GMImage.OilPaint
functions.description: Oilpaint image (image looks like oil painting)

functions.name: GMImage.Opacity
functions.description: Set or attenuate the opacity channel in the image. If the image pixels are opaque then they are set to the specified opacity value, otherwise they are blended with the supplied opacity value.  The value of opacity ranges from 0 (completely opaque) to MaxRGB (65535). The defines OpaqueOpacity and TransparentOpacity are available to specify completely opaque or completely transparent, respectively.

functions.name: GMImage.Opaque
functions.description: Change color of opaque pixel to specified pen color.

parameters.name: opaqueColor
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

parameters.name: penColor
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

functions.name: GMImage.Ping
functions.description: Pings an image to query metadata. Ping is similar to read except only enough of the image is read to determine the image columns, rows, and file size. See GMImage.GetWidth , GMImage.GetHeight and GMImage.GetXMP . This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Spec
parameters.description: The image specification. e.g. native file path

";

( "
GMImage.GetHeight
";
$img
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Size" ;
$w
& " x " &
$h
]
Else
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed" ;
$r
]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Release
";
$img
) ]

functions.name: GMImage.PopClipPath
functions.description: Pop (terminate) clip path definition started by GMImage.PushClipPath .

functions.description: Queries the list of available profiles. Profile identifiers include usually "8BIM", "EXIF, "ICM", "IPTC" or "XMP". See also GMImage.GetProfile to read profiles and GMImage.SetProfile to set profiles.

r =

("
GMImage.Release
"; img)
]; xmp)
Example result:
EXIF
ICM
APP10

   path = "/Users/cs/test/IMG_5858.jpg";

functions.name: GMImage.PurgeTemporaryFiles
functions.description: Asks GraphicsMagick to purge all temp files. The plugin checks if images are in use and refuses to purge if there are current image references. If you process images in a batch and you finish your work, you can call this method after freeing the last image to clear temp files.

functions.name: GMImage.PushClipPath
functions.description: Push (create) clip path definition with new name. Clip patch definition consists of subsequent drawing commands, terminated by GMImage.PopClipPath .

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the new clippath.

functions.name: GMImage.Quantize
functions.description: Quantize image (reduce number of colors). See GMImage.SetQuantizeColorSpace , GMImage.SetQuantizeColors , GMImage.SetQuantizeDither and GMImage.SetQuantizeTreeDepth for options.

examples.label: Quantize picture:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$BigImageRef
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Test::BigImage
) ]
# reduce to 20 colors, so we see the effect. Normally 256
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.SetQuantizeColors
";
$BigImageRef
; 20) // 256 ) ]
# Enable dithering, turn off to really see the effect
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.SetQuantizeDither
";
$BigImageRef
; 1 ) ]
# Quantize to palette colors
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.SetQuantizeColorSpace
";
$BigImageRef
; 4 /* palette */ ) ]
# Do it!
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("GMImage.Quantize";
$BigImageRef
) ]
# Type should now be 4 for palette
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.GetType
";
$BigImageRef
) ]
# save as GIF
Set Field [
Test::Result
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToGIFContainer
";
$BigImageRef
) ]
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$BigImageRef
) ]

    <Text> Do it!</Text>

functions.name: GMImage.QuantumDepth
functions.description: Queries quantum depth of GraphicsMagick. This is bit depth used to store one component of a pixel. The MBS Plugin can be build with 8 or 16 bit.

functions.name: GMImage.Raise
functions.description: Raise image (lighten or darken the edges of an image to give a 3-D raised or lowered effect)

parameters.name: Geometry
parameters.description: The rectangle. Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200". With an optional offset "100x200+10+20". For percent values, add a percent on the end, for % values add a %: "100x200%!"

examples.label: Raises the border of an image:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 
$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$ScaleResult = MBS("GMImage.Raise";$Image); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $Image); 
$Error = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 
$Result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Raise an image:
examples.text_code:
# load images...
Set Variable [
$Image
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.NewFromFile
"; "/Users/cs/Pictures/IMG_3625.jpg") ]
# raise image
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.Raise";
$Image
) ]
# and save to file
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToFile
";
$Image
; "/Users/cs/Pictures/output.jpg") ]
# free memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Release
";
$Image
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> load images...</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.NewFromFile"; "/Users/cs/Pictures/IMG_3625.jpg") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> raise image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.Raise"; $Image) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and save to file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.WriteToFile"; $Image; "/Users/cs/Pictures/output.jpg") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> free memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.Release"; $Image) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.RandomThreshold
functions.description: Random threshold image. Changes the value of individual pixels based on the intensity of each pixel compared to a random threshold.  The result is a low-contrast, two color image.  The thresholds_ argument is a geometry containing LOWxHIGH thresholds.  If the string contains 2x2, 3x3, or 4x4, then an ordered dither of order 2, 3, or 4 will be performed instead.  If a ChannelType argument is specified then only the specified channel is altered.  This is a very fast alternative to 'quantize' based dithering. This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 GMImage.Raise - GMImage.RandomThresholdChannel 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: thresholds
parameters.description: The rectangle. Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200". With an optional offset "100x200+10+20". For percent values, add a percent on the end, for % values add a %: "100x200%!"

functions.name: GMImage.RandomThresholdChannel
functions.description: Random threshold image. Changes the value of individual pixels based on the intensity of each pixel compared to a random threshold.  The result is a low-contrast, two color image.  The thresholds_ argument is a geometry containing LOWxHIGH thresholds.  If the string contains 2x2, 3x3, or 4x4, then an ordered dither of order 2, 3, or 4 will be performed instead.  If a ChannelType argument is specified then only the specified channel is altered.  This is a very fast alternative to 'quantize' based dithering. This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 GMImage.RandomThreshold - GMImage.Read 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: thresholds
parameters.description: The rectangle. Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200". With an optional offset "100x200+10+20". For percent values, add a percent on the end, for % values add a %: "100x200%!"

parameters.name: ChannelType
parameters.example: One of the following values: RedChannel = 1 (RGB Red channel), CyanChannel = 2 (CMYK Cyan channel), GreenChannel = 3 (RGB Green channel), MagentaChannel = 4 (CMYK Magenta channel), BlueChannel = 5 (RGB Blue channel), YellowChannel = 6 (CMYK Yellow channel), OpacityChannel = 7 (Opacity channel), BlackChannel = 8 (CMYK Black (K) channel), MatteChannel = 9 (Same as Opacity channel (deprecated)), AllChannels = 10 (Color channels), GrayChannel = 11 (Color channels represent an intensity.)

functions.name: GMImage.Read
functions.description: Read single image frame into current object. This allows you to directly specify the coders to use and include options. e.g. gradient coder takes a range of two colors to create gradient. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Spec
parameters.description: The image specification. e.g. native file path

parameters.name: Geometry
parameters.description: The image size. Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200". With an optional offset "100x200+10+20". For percent values, add a percent on the end, for % values add a %: "100x200%!"

functions.name: GMImage.ReadContainer
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.ReadContainer"; ImageRef; Data { ; Geometry; Depth; Magick } )
functions.description: Read single image frame into current object. This allows you to set options before reading in image. You may want to use GMImage.SetBackgroundColor , GMImage.SetMagick , GMImage.SetFileName or other settings before calling this.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: The container value, either a variable or a container field.

parameters.name: Geometry
parameters.description: The rectangle. Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200". Can be empty.

parameters.name: Magick
parameters.description: The codec to use to read file.

examples.label: Convert a SVG image to PNG with alpha channel:
examples.text_code:
# Load image from container
Set Variable [
$image
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.New
"; "500x500"; "transparent" ) ]
# turn on alpha channel and set background to transparent
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.SetType
";
$image
; 7 ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.SetBackgroundColor
";
$Image
; "transparent" ) ]
# read SVG
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.ReadContainer";
$image
;
SVG to PNG::InputFile
; "500x500"; 0; "SVG" ) ]
# Output to container and release image
Set Field [
SVG to PNG::OutputFile
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
";
$image
; "test.png") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Free
";
$image
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load image from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.New"; "500x500"; "transparent" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> turn on alpha channel and set background to transparent</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetType"; $image; 7 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetBackgroundColor"; $Image; "transparent" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.ReadContainer"; $image; SVG to PNG::InputFile; "500x500"; 0; "SVG" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Output to container and release image</Text>

  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $image; "test.png") </Calculation>
    <Field table="SVG to PNG" id="99999" name="OutputFile "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Free"; $image) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>

functions.name: GMImage.ReduceNoise
functions.description: Reduce noise in image using a noise peak elimination filter. This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 GMImage.ReadContainer - GMImage.Release 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: GMImage.Release
functions.description: Releases the image from memory. Please call this function when you are done with the image to free memory. The plugin doesn't know whether you need the image later, so it can't free the memory for you. You can use GMImage.ImageCount to find out how many images are open in memory. Used to be named GMImage.Release before. You need to release all image references returned by functions GMImage.New , GMImage.NewFromBase64 , GMImage.NewFromContainer , GMImage.NewFromFile , GMImage.NewFromHex , GMImage.NewImagesFromContainer , GMImage.NewImagesFromFile , GMImage.Copy , GMImage.GetClipMask , MenuItem.GetImage , MenuItem.GetMixedStateImage , MenuItem.GetOffStateImage , MenuItem.GetOnStateImage , Twain.CurrentImage , and Barcode.Generate .

examples.label: Show with and height
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 
$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$Width = MBS("GMImage.GetWidth";$Image); 
$Height = MBS("GMImage.GetHeight";$Image); 
$Error = MBS("GMImage.Destroy";$Image)
]; 
$Width & " x " & $Height )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.Repage
functions.description: Resets the image page canvas and position.

functions.name: GMImage.ReplaceColor
functions.description: Replaces a color with another color value for the whole image. Match is exact, so no fuzzy matching unless you pass tolerance parameter.

parameters.name: color1
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

parameters.name: color2
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

parameters.name: Tolerance
parameters.description: The tolerance to allow for a match. Pass 1 to have one channel be matched if value is one away, e.g. 128 is accepted if searched for 127 or 129. Default is 0. Since MBS Plugin 9.x uses 16-bit values, please multiple by 257 to expand older 8-bit values to 16-bit.

; "logo.png" )]
Set Variable [
$r

GMImage.Release
";
$img
)]

functions.name: GMImage.Resize
functions.description: Resize image, specifying geometry, filter, and blur. If filter or blur is not specified, we use image defaults.

parameters.name: Geometry
parameters.description: The rectangle to use. Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200". With an optional offset "100x200+10+20". For percent values, add a percent on the end, for % values add a %: "100x200%!"

parameters.name: FilterTypes
parameters.description: Which filter to use. Can be one of Undefined, Point, Box, Triangle, Hermite, Hanning, Hamming, Blackman, Gaussian, Quadratic, Cubic, Catrom, Mitchell, Lanczos, Bessel, or Sinc.

parameters.name: Blur
parameters.description: The blur radius. Only used if filter is specified.

functions.name: GMImage.Rotate
functions.description: Rotate image by specified number of degrees. Positive angles rotate the image in a clockwise direction while negative angles rotate counter-clockwise.

examples.label: Rotate with script and error checking:
examples.text_code:
# Read image into variable
Set Variable [
$image
; Value:
LiveStream::Image
]
If [ IsEmpty (
$image
) ]
# nothing to do
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
# load image
Set Variable [
$ImageRef
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
$image
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to load image." ;
$ImageRef
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
# rotate it
Set Variable [
$RotationResult
; Value:
MBS
("GMImage.Rotate";
$ImageRef
; 90) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$ImageRef
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to rotate image." ;
$RotationResult
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
# write back
Set Variable [
$NewImage
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
";
$ImageRef
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$ImageRef
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to rotate image." ;
$NewImage
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
# if not error, put it back to record
Set Field [
LiveStream::Image
;
$NewImage
]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$ImageRef
) ]

functions.name: GMImage.Rotating
functions.description: Set rotation to use when drawing.

functions.name: GMImage.Rows
functions.description: Returns the image height.

functions.name: GMImage.Sample
functions.description: Resize image by using pixel sampling algorithm.

parameters.name: Geometry
parameters.description: The rectangle. Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200". With an optional offset "100x200+10+20". For percent values, add a percent on the end, for % values add a %: "100x200%!"

functions.name: GMImage.Scale
functions.description: Resize image by using simple ratio algorithm. GraphicsMagick has added a number of qualifiers to the standard geometry string for use when resizing images. The form of an extended geometry string is "<width>x<height>{+-}<xoffset>{+-}<yoffset>{%}{!}{<}{>}". Extended geometry strings should only be used when resizing an image. Using an extended geometry string for other applications may cause the API call to fail. The available qualifiers are shown in the following table:

parameters.name: Geometry
parameters.description: The new size. Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200".

examples.label: Scales the image to 50% with calculating ourself
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 
$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$Width = MBS("GMImage.GetWidth";$Image); 
$Height = MBS("GMImage.GetHeight";$Image); 
$Width = $Width/2;
$Height = $Height/2;
$ScaleResult = MBS("GMImage.Scale";$Image; $Width & "x" & $Height); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $Image); 
$Error = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 
$Result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Scale with error checking:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 
Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; MyContainer); 
// did loading work?
Error = MBS("IsError");
// don't scale if it failed
ScaleResult = If(Error; ""; MBS("GMImage.Scale"; Image; "500>")); 
NewName = "Preview.png" ;
// pass through old container, if we can't load it.
NewImage = If(Error; MyContainer; MBS("GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; Image; NewName));

ReleaseResult = MBS("GMImage.Release"; Image)
]; 
NewImage)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.Scaling
functions.description: Apply scaling in x and y direction while drawing objects.

functions.name: GMImage.Segment
functions.description: Segments the image. Segment (coalesce similar image components) by analyzing the histograms of the color components and identifying units that are homogeneous with the fuzzy c-means technique.  Also uses QuantizeColorSpace and Verbose image attributes

functions.name: GMImage.SetAdjoin
functions.description: Set adjoin value. Join images into a single multi-image file

functions.name: GMImage.SetAnimationDelay
functions.description: Sets the animation delay. Time in 1/100ths of a second which must expire before displaying the next image in an animated sequence. Time in 1/100ths of a second (0 to 65535) which must expire before displaying the next image in an animated sequence. This option is useful for regulating the animation of a sequence of GIF images within Netscape.

functions.name: GMImage.SetAnimationIterations
functions.description: Sets the Number of iterations to loop an animation (e.g. Netscape loop extension) for.

parameters.name: iterations
parameters.description: the Number of iterations to loop an animation for

functions.name: GMImage.SetAntiAlias
functions.description: Sets whether to Anti-alias Postscript and TrueType fonts (default true)

functions.name: GMImage.SetAttribute
functions.description: Set a named attribute. GraphicsMagick does not support writing attributes back to files, so this only stores new value in memory for GMImage.GetAttribute to query.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding. Default is UTF-8. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.name: GMImage.SetBackgroundColor
functions.description: Sets the Image background color.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

$image
;
SVG to PNG::InputFile
; "500x500"; 0; "SVG" ) ]

SVG to PNG::OutputFile
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
";
$image
; "test.png") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:

("
GMImage.Free
";
$image
) ]

examples.label: Removes alpha channel:
examples.text_code:
# Load from container
Set Variable [
$BigImageRef
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Test::BigImage
) ]
# set the color to use for transparent areas
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("GMImage.SetBackgroundColor";
$BigImageRef
; "white") ]
# and remove transparency
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Flatten
";
$BigImageRef
) ]
# Store output and release the image
Set Field [
Test::Result
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer
";
$BigImageRef
) ]
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Free
";
$BigImageRef
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; Test::BigImage) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$BigImageRef</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set the color to use for transparent areas</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetBackgroundColor"; $BigImageRef; "white") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and remove transparency</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Flatten"; $BigImageRef) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Store output and release the image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer"; $BigImageRef ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Test" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Free";$BigImageRef) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Error</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.SetBackgroundTexture
functions.description: Sets the Name of texture image to tile onto the image background

functions.name: GMImage.SetBorderColor
functions.description: Sets the Image border color. You can set this and call GMImage.Border to add border with given color.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

examples.label: Add whitespace on top and bottom of image:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 

$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$r = MBS( "GMImage.SetBorderColor"; $Image; "white" );
$r = MBS("GMImage.Border";$Image; "0x100"); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $Image); 

$r = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 

$Result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.SetBoxColor
functions.description: Text bounding-box base color (default none).

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

functions.name: GMImage.SetChannelDepth
functions.description: Set modulus channel depth

parameters.name: ChannelType
parameters.description: One of the following values: RedChannel = 1 (RGB Red channel), CyanChannel = 2 (CMYK Cyan channel), GreenChannel = 3 (RGB Green channel), MagentaChannel = 4 (CMYK Magenta channel), BlueChannel = 5 (RGB Blue channel), YellowChannel = 6 (CMYK Yellow channel), OpacityChannel = 7 (Opacity channel), BlackChannel = 8 (CMYK Black (K) channel), MatteChannel = 9 (Same as Opacity channel (deprecated)), AllChannels = 10 (Color channels), GrayChannel = 11 (Color channels represent an intensity.)

functions.name: GMImage.SetClassType
functions.description: Sets the Image class. setting a DirectClass image to PseudoClass will result in the loss of color information if the number of colors in the image is greater than the maximum palette size (either 256 or 65536 entries depending on the value of QuantumDepth when ImageMagick was built). Value can be DirectClass = 1 or PseudoClass = 2.

functions.name: GMImage.SetColorFuzz
functions.description: Sets the color fuzz. Colors within this distance are considered equal. Used for example with GMImage.Trim function. Range is from 0 to 65535 with zero being no fuzz and 65535 to match all colors. Useful range may be around 50*257.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: a float number. Since MBS Plugin 9.x uses 16-bit values, please multiple by 257 to expand older 8-bit values to 16-bit.
