examples.label: Make a picture transparent:
examples.text_code:
#load a picture
Set Variable [
$image
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Test::BigImage
)]
#white in RGB
Set Variable [
$f
; Value:"white"]
#Replace all white with nearly white
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.ReplaceColor
";
$image
;
$f
; "white"; 0)]
#now flood fill it with 5 as tolerance
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.SetColorFuzz";
$image
; 5 )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.FloodFillColorXY
";
$image
; 0; 0;
$f
)]
#now set back fuzz and make transparent using white
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.SetColorFuzz";
$image
; 0 )]
Set Variable [
$imagecolor
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Transparent
";
$image
;
$f
)]
#write back to container
Set Field [
Test::Result
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
";
$image
;)]
#cleanup
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$image
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>load a picture</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.NewFromContainer"; Test::BigImage)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>white in RGB</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"white"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$f</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Replace all white with nearly white</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.ReplaceColor"; $image; $f; "white"; 0)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>now flood fill it with 5 as tolerance</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetColorFuzz"; $image; 5 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.FloodFillColorXY"; $image; 0; 0; $f )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>now set back fuzz and make transparent using white</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetColorFuzz"; $image; 0 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.Transparent"; $image; $f)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$imagecolor</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>write back to container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $image;)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Test" id="99999" name="Result"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMimage.Release";$image)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Error</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.SetColorMap
functions.description: Sets an entry in the color map.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: Position of the entry. Zero based.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

functions.name: GMImage.SetColorMapSize
functions.description: Sets the Colormap size (number of colormap entries)

functions.name: GMImage.SetColorSpace
functions.description: Sets the Image Color Space.

parameters.name: ColorSpaceType
parameters.description: UndefinedColorspace = 0, RGBColorspace = 1 (Plain old RGB colorspace), GRAYColorspace = 2 (Plain old full-range grayscale), TransparentColorspace = 3 (RGB but preserve matte channel during quantize), OHTAColorspace = 4, XYZColorspace = 5 (CIE XYZ), YCCColorspace = 6 (Kodak PhotoCD PhotoYCC), YIQColorspace = 7, YPbPrColorspace = 8, YUVColorspace = 9, CMYKColorspace = 10 (Cyan, magenta, yellow, black, alpha), sRGBColorspace = 11 (Kodak PhotoCD sRGB), HSLColorspace = 12 (Hue, saturation, luminosity), HWBColorspace = 13 (Hue, whiteness, blackness), LABColorspace = 14 (LAB colorspace not supported yet other than via lcms), CineonLogRGBColorspace = 15 (RGB data with Cineon Log scaling, 2.048 density range), Rec601LumaColorspace = 16 (Luma (Y) according to ITU-R 601), Rec601YCbCrColorspace = 17 (YCbCr according to ITU-R 601), Rec709LumaColorspace = 18 (Luma (Y) according to ITU-R 709), Rec709YCbCrColorspace = 19 (YCbCr according to ITU-R 709)

functions.name: GMImage.SetComment
functions.description: Sets the image comment.

functions.name: GMImage.SetCompose
functions.description: Sets the Composition operator to be used when composition is implicitly used (such as for image flattening).

parameters.name: CompositeOperator
parameters.description: UndefinedCompositeOp = 0, OverCompositeOp = 1, InCompositeOp = 2, OutCompositeOp = 3, AtopCompositeOp = 4, XorCompositeOp = 5, PlusCompositeOp = 6, MinusCompositeOp = 7, AddCompositeOp = 8, SubtractCompositeOp = 9, DifferenceCompositeOp = 10, MultiplyCompositeOp = 11, BumpmapCompositeOp = 12, CopyCompositeOp = 13, CopyRedCompositeOp = 14, CopyGreenCompositeOp = 15, CopyBlueCompositeOp = 16, CopyOpacityCompositeOp = 17, ClearCompositeOp = 18, DissolveCompositeOp = 19, DisplaceCompositeOp = 20, ModulateCompositeOp = 21, ThresholdCompositeOp = 22, NoCompositeOp = 23, DarkenCompositeOp = 24, LightenCompositeOp = 25, HueCompositeOp = 26, SaturateCompositeOp = 27, ColorizeCompositeOp = 28, LuminizeCompositeOp = 29, CopyCyanCompositeOp = 32, CopyMagentaCompositeOp = 33, CopyYellowCompositeOp = 34, CopyBlackCompositeOp = 35, DivideCompositeOp = 36.

functions.name: GMImage.SetCompressType
functions.description: Sets the compression type. CompressionType is used to express the desired compression type when encoding an image. Be aware that most image types only support a sub-set of the available compression types. If the compression type specified is incompatible with the image, GraphicsMagick selects a compression type compatible with the image type. GIF is always LZW compressed, PNG is always zip compressed.

parameters.name: CompressionType
parameters.description: UndefinedCompression = 0, NoCompression = 1, BZipCompression = 2, FaxCompression = 3, Group4Compression = 4, JPEGCompression = 5, LosslessJPEGCompression = 6, LZWCompression = 7, RLECompression = 8, ZipCompression = 9

examples.label: Convert image to 1 bit TIFF:
examples.text_code:
# Load from container
Set Variable [
$Image
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
GraphicsMagick::image
) ]
# make black and white with threshold
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Threshold
";
$Image
; 127) ]
# mark as monochrome image
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetMonochrome
";
$Image
; 1) ]
# set type to 1 for black/white
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetType
";
$Image
; 1) ]
# set to use TIFF for compression
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetMagick
";
$Image
; "TIFF") ]
# set to use CCITT 4 Fax compression
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("GMImage.SetCompressType";
$Image
; 4) ]
# and write to a file
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.WriteToFile
";
$Image
; "/tmp/test.tif") ]
# cleanup
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$Image
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> make black and white with threshold</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Threshold";$Image; 127) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> mark as monochrome image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetMonochrome";$Image; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set type to 1 for black/white</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetType";$Image; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set to use TIFF for compression</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetMagick";$Image; "TIFF") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set to use CCITT 4 Fax compression</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetCompressType";$Image; 4) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and write to a file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.WriteToFile"; $Image; "/tmp/test.tif") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Release";$Image) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Error</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.SetDebug
functions.description: Enables printing of debug messages from ImageMagick

functions.name: GMImage.SetDefineSet
functions.description: Sets a define value. Tagged image format define. Similar to the get/setdefineValue() functions except that passing the flag value 'true' creates a value-less define with that format and key. Passing the flag value 'false' removes any existing matching definition. The method returns 'true' if a matching key exists, and 'false' if no matching key exists.

functions.name: GMImage.SetDefineValue
functions.description: Sets a value. Tagged image format define (set/access coder-specific option) The magick option specifies the coder the define applies to.  The key option provides the key specific to that coder.  The value option provides the value to set (if any). See the get/set defineSet functions if the key must be removed entirely.

functions.name: GMImage.SetDensity
functions.description: Sets the Vertical and horizontal resolution in pixels of the image. Please use GMImage.SetResolutionUnits to define units.

parameters.name: Geometry
parameters.description: The rectangle. Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200".

examples.label: Set resolution and remove all metadata with different resolution:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 

$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image ); 
$r = MBS("GMImage.SetDensity"; $Image; "72x72") ;
$r = MBS(  "GMImage.SetProfile"; $Image; "EXIF"; "" ) ;
$r = MBS(  "GMImage.SetProfile"; $Image; "IPTC"; "" ) ;
$r = MBS(  "GMImage.SetProfile"; $Image; "XMP"; "" ) ;
$Result = MBS("GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer"; $Image); 
$Error = MBS("GMImage.Release";$Image)
]; 

$Result

)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Standardize all images to a maximum of 800x800 points @ 144 dpi which is 1600x1600 pixels:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$ImageRef
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Test::BigImage
)]
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Scale
";
$ImageRef
; "1600x1600") // maximum size in pixels ]
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetResolutionUnits
";
$ImageRef
; 1) // resolution is in DPI ]
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("GMImage.SetDensity";
$ImageRef
; "144x144") // set the DPI value ]
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetQuality
";
$ImageRef
; 90) // save as JPEG with 90% ]
Set Field [
Test::Result
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer
";
$ImageRef
)]
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$ImageRef
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; Test::BigImage)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ImageRef</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Scale"; $ImageRef; "1600x1600") // maximum size in pixels </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetResolutionUnits"; $ImageRef; 1) // resolution is in DPI </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetDensity"; $ImageRef; "144x144") // set the DPI value </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetQuality"; $ImageRef; 90) // save as JPEG with 90% </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer"; $ImageRef )</Calculation>
    <Field table="Test" id="99999" name="Result"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Release";$ImageRef)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Error</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.SetDepth
functions.description: Sets the Image depth (bits allocated to red/green/blue components)

functions.name: GMImage.SetEXIFOrientation
functions.description: Sets orientation for EXIF. Changing orientation may need to set orientation via GMImage.SetOrientation and GMImage.SetEXIFOrientation. With a JPEG you have orientation both in JPEG header and in EXIF metadata.

functions.name: GMImage.SetEndian
functions.description: Sets the Endianness (little like Intel or big like SPARC) for image formats which support endian-specific options.

functions.name: GMImage.SetFileName
functions.description: Sets the Image file name.

functions.name: GMImage.SetFillColor
functions.description: Sets the Color to use when filling drawn objects.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

examples.label: Set red fill color:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "GMImage.SetFillColor";
$img
; "red" )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetFillColor"; $img; "red" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.name: FilterType

examples.label: Annotate an image on an Ubuntu FileMaker Server:
examples.text_code:
Go to Layout [ “Annotate Test” ; Animation: None ]
Go to Record/Request/Page [ First ]
Set Variable [
$ref
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.New
"; "200x200"; "white" ) ]
# Settings for the font
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetFontPointSize
";
$ref
; 12) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("GMImage.SetFont";
$ref
; "@/usr/share/fonts/truetype/ubuntu/Ubuntu-R.ttf") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetFillColor
";
$ref
; "RGB 0 0 0") ]
# Write the text to the picture
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Annotate
";
$ref
; "Hello World"; "100x200+10+40"; 1) ]
# store the picture in the field
Set Field [
WebHook Chat::File
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
";
$ref
) ]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
# Free image
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Free
";
$ref
) ]

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetFont"; $ref; "@/usr/share/fonts/truetype/ubuntu/Ubuntu-R.ttf") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetFillColor"; $ref; "RGB 0 0 0") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Write the text to the picture</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Annotate"; $ref; "Hello World"; "100x200+10+40"; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

    <Name>$Error</Name>
  </Step>

functions.name: GMImage.SetFontPointSize
functions.description: Sets the font point size.

functions.name: GMImage.SetFontStretch
functions.description: Set font stretch.

parameters.name: Stretch
parameters.description: The font stretch. Can be Normal, UltraCondensed, ExtraCondensed, Condensed, SemiCondensed, SemiExpanded, Expanded, ExtraExpanded, UltraExpanded or Any.

functions.name: GMImage.SetFontStyle
functions.description: Set font style.

parameters.name: Style
parameters.description: The font style to use. Can be Normal, Italic, Oblique or Any.

functions.name: GMImage.SetFontWeight
functions.description: Sets font weight.

parameters.name: Weight
parameters.description: The font weight in range from 0 to 1000. 400 is normal and 800 bold.

functions.name: GMImage.SetGifDisposeMethod
functions.description: Sets the GIF disposal method.

functions.name: GMImage.SetICCColorProfile
functions.description: Sets the ICC color profile. Supplied via a Blob since GraphicsMagick do not currently support formatting this data structure directly. If there is not already an ICC color profile, the profile is merely attached to the image without transforming the pixels. If there is already an ICC color profile (the source profile), the pixels are translated according to the source and target profiles, and the existing profile is replaced with the target profile. Also see GMImage.SetRenderingIntent , which allows specifying the rendering intent if the profile is executed. Specifications for ICC color profiles and their usage are available from the International Color Consortium for the format of ICC color profiles.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: a Container field value with an embedded file containing the profile.

examples.label: Removes an existing profile:
examples.text_code:

("GMImage.SetICCColorPRofile";
$Image
; "")

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.SetLogEventMask
functions.description: Set log event mask. Defines which events are logged. By default logging goes to stderr, so you may need to look into the log files on the FileMaker Server for server side usage and run FileMaker via Terminal on macOS to see logs there. List of events includes: none, Configure, Annotate, Render, Transform, Locale, Coder, X11, Cache, Blob, Deprecate, User, Resource, TemporaryFile, Exception, Option, Information, Warning, Error, FatalError and All.

parameters.name: EventMask
parameters.description: The list of events to monitor, comma separated.

functions.name: GMImage.SetMagick

examples.label: Convert image to 1 bit TIFF:
examples.text_code:
# Load from container
Set Variable [
$Image
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
GraphicsMagick::image
);  ]
# make black and white with threshold
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Threshold
";
$Image
; 127);  ]
# mark as monochrome image
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetMonochrome
";
$Image
; 1);  ]
# set type to 1 for black/white
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetType
";
$Image
; 1);  ]
# set to use TIFF for compression
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("GMImage.SetMagick";
$Image
; "TIFF");  ]
# set to use CCITT 4 Fax compression
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:

GMImage.SetCompressType
";
$Image
; 4);  ]
# and write to a file

$Result
; Value:

("
GMImage.WriteToFile
";
$Image
; "/tmp/test.tif");  ]
# cleanup
Set Variable [

; Value:

("
GMImage.Release
";
$Image
) ]

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

functions.name: GMImage.SetMatteColor
functions.description: Sets the Transparent color.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

functions.name: GMImage.SetModulusDepth
functions.description: Sets the Image modulus depth. The minimum number of bits required to support red/green/blue components without loss of accuracy.

functions.name: GMImage.SetPage
functions.description: Sets the Preferred size and location of an image canvas.

functions.name: GMImage.SetPixelColor
functions.description: set pixel color at location x & y.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: The horizontal position. Range from 0 to GMImage.GetWidth -1.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: The vertical position. Range from 0 to GMImage.GetHeight -1.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

functions.name: GMImage.SetProfile
functions.description: Add or remove a named profile to/from the image. Remove the profile by passing an empty container field. Valid names are "*", "8BIM", "ICM", "IPTC", "EXIF", or a user/format-defined profile name.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The string with the name of the profile.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: The container field value with the embedded profile file.

( "GMImage.SetProfile";
$newImage
; "IPTC";
$data
) ]
# write to file
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToFile
";
$newImage
; "/Users/cs/Desktop/Import.jpg" ) ]
# and free memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Release
";
$newImage
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Release
";
$img
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> load a picture</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; IPTC::InputImage) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$newImage</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.GetProfile"; $img; "IPTC" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$data</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetProfile"; $newImage; "IPTC"; $data ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> write to file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.WriteToFile"; $newImage; "/Users/cs/Desktop/Import.jpg" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and free memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.Release"; $newImage) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.Release"; $img) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.SetQuality
functions.description: Sets the JPEG/MIFF/PNG compression level (default 75).

parameters.name: Quality
parameters.description: a number in range of 0 to 100.

examples.label: Recompresses the image with JPEG compressor and a really bad quality:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 
$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.SetQuality";$Image; 5); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer"; $Image); 
$Error = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 
$Result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.SetQuantizeColorSpace
functions.description: Sets the Colorspace to quantize in.

parameters.name: ColorSpaceType
parameters.description: UndefinedColorspace = 0, RGBColorspace = 1 (Plain old RGB colorspace), GRAYColorspace = 2 (Plain old full-range grayscale), TransparentColorspace = 3 (RGB but preserve matte channel during quantize), OHTAColorspace = 4, XYZColorspace = 5 (CIE XYZ), YCCColorspace = 6 (Kodak PhotoCD PhotoYCC), YIQColorspace = 7, YPbPrColorspace = 8, YUVColorspace = 9, CMYKColorspace = 10 (Cyan, magenta, yellow, black, alpha), sRGBColorspace = 11 (Kodak PhotoCD sRGB), HSLColorspace = 12 (Hue, saturation, luminosity), HWBColorspace = 13 (Hue, whiteness, blackness), LABColorspace = 14 (LAB colorspace not supported yet other than via lcms), CineonLogRGBColorspace = 15 (RGB data with Cineon Log scaling, 2.048 density range), Rec601LumaColorspace = 16 (Luma (Y) according to ITU-R 601), Rec601YCbCrColorspace = 17 (YCbCr according to ITU-R 601), Rec709LumaColorspace = 18 (Luma (Y) according to ITU-R 709), Rec709YCbCrColorspace = 19 (YCbCr according to ITU-R 709)

functions.name: GMImage.SetQuantizeColors
functions.description: Sets the Maximum number of colors to quantize to

parameters.name: Colors
parameters.description: Maximum number of colors to quantize to

functions.name: GMImage.SetQuantizeDither
functions.description: Sets whether to dither image during quantization (default true).

functions.name: GMImage.SetQuantizeTreeDepth
functions.description: Sets the Quantization tree-depth.

functions.name: GMImage.SetQuiet
functions.description: Sets quiet setting. If enabled, suppress all warning messages. Error messages are still reported.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 for quiet.

functions.name: GMImage.SetRenderingIntent
functions.description: Sets the type of rendering intent

functions.name: GMImage.SetResolutionUnits
functions.description: Sets which units are used for image resolution.

examples.label: Convert and Scale:
examples.text_code:
# Load image from container
Set Variable [
$image
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
SVG to PNG::InputFile
) ]
Set Variable [
$name
; Value:
MBS
( "
Files.FileName
"; LiesAlsText(
SVG to PNG::InputFile
) ) ]
# Scale if parameters are filled in fields
If [ IsEmpty (
SVG to PNG::Target
Width in Pixel ) ]
If [ IsEmpty (
SVG to PNG::Target
Height in Pixel ) ]
# both empty, do nothing
Else
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Scale
";
$image
; "99999x" &
SVG to PNG::Target
Height in Pixel & ">") ]
End If
Else
If [ IsEmpty (
SVG to PNG::Target
Height in Pixel ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Scale
";
$image
;
SVG to PNG::Target
Width in Pixel& ">" ) ]
Else
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
(  "
GMImage.Scale
";  // function
$image
;   //
pass in picture SVG to PNG::Target
Width in Pixel & // width  "x" &  //
by SVG to PNG::Target
Height in Pixel & // height ">" // scale only if it will get smaller ) ]
End If
End If
# If a resolution is defined, put it in
If [ NOT IsEmpty (
SVG to PNG::Resolution
in DPI ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.SetResolutionUnits";
$image
; 1 /* pixel per inch */ ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.SetDensity
";
$image
;
SVG to PNG::Resolution
in DPI & "x" &
SVG to PNG::Resolution
in DPI) ]
End If
# Output to container
Set Field [
SVG to PNG::OutputFile
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
";
$image
;
$name
& ".png") ]
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$image
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load image from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.NewFromContainer"; SVG to PNG::InputFile ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.FileName"; LiesAlsText(SVG to PNG::InputFile) ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Scale if parameters are filled in fields</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> IsEmpty ( SVG to PNG::Target Width in Pixel ) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> IsEmpty ( SVG to PNG::Target Height in Pixel ) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> both empty, do nothing</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.Scale"; $image; "99999x" & SVG to PNG::Target Height in Pixel & ">") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> IsEmpty ( SVG to PNG::Target Height in Pixel ) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.Scale"; $image; SVG to PNG::Target Width in Pixel& ">" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS(  "GMImage.Scale";  // function $image;   // pass in picture SVG to PNG::Target Width in Pixel & // width  "x" &  // by SVG to PNG::Target Height in Pixel & // height ">" // scale only if it will get smaller ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> If a resolution is defined, put it in</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> NOT IsEmpty ( SVG to PNG::Resolution in DPI ) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetResolutionUnits"; $image; 1 /* pixel per inch */ ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetDensity"; $image; SVG to PNG::Resolution in DPI & "x" & SVG to PNG::Resolution in DPI) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Output to container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $image; $name & ".png") </Calculation>
    <Field table="SVG to PNG" id="99999" name="OutputFile "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMimage.Release"; $image) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

$ImageRef

GMImage.Release

$ImageRef

functions.name: GMImage.SetScene
functions.description: Sets the image scene number

functions.name: GMImage.SetSize
functions.description: Sets the Width and height of a raw image Width and height of a raw image (an image which does not support width and height information). Size may also be used to affect the image size read from a multi-resolution format (e.g. Photo CD, JBIG, or JPEG) To scale images, please use GMImage.Scale function. If you set a size, that doesn't match the pixel cache size, you may later see an error "Pixel cache dimensions incompatible with image dimensions".

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: The rectangle. A Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200". With an optional offset "100x200+10+20". For percent values, add a percent on the end, for % values add a %: "100x200%!"

functions.name: GMImage.SetStrokeAntiAlias
functions.description: Enables/disables stroke anti-aliasing.

functions.name: GMImage.SetStrokeColor
functions.description: Sets the Color to use when drawing object outlines. The plugin has a list so some colors can be simply named like "red".

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

examples.label: Set color to red:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "GMImage.SetStrokeColor";
$img
; "red" )
examples.xml_code:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.SetStrokeDashArray
functions.description: Specify the pattern of dashes and gaps used to stroke paths. The strokeDashArray represents an array of numbers that specify the lengths of alternating dashes and gaps in pixels. If an odd number of values is provided, then the list of values is repeated to yield an even number of values. A typical strokeDashArray array might contain the members 5 3 2.

parameters.name: Pattern
parameters.description: The list of pattern numbers. can be separated by space or newline.

examples.label: Set a dash line pattern:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "GMImage.SetStrokeDashArray";
$ImageRef
; "5 3 2" )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetStrokeDashArray"; $ImageRef; "5 3 2" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.SetStrokeDashOffset
functions.description: Sets the stroke dash offset. While drawing using a dash pattern, specify distance into the dash pattern to start the dash (default 0).

functions.name: GMImage.SetStrokeLineCap
functions.description: Specify the shape to be used at the end of open subpaths when they are stroked. See also GMImage.SetStrokeLineJoin .

functions.name: GMImage.SetStrokeMiterLimit
functions.description: Sets the miter limit. When two line segments meet at a sharp angle and miter joins have been specified for 'lineJoin', it is possible for the miter to extend far beyond the thickness of the line stroking the path. The miterLimit' imposes a limit on the ratio of the miter length to the 'lineWidth'. The default value of this parameter is 4.

functions.name: GMImage.SetStrokeWidth
functions.description: Sets the Stroke width for drawing vector objects (default one) If you have an open path, the command is added there for later drawing with GMImage.DrawPath .

functions.name: GMImage.SetSubImage
functions.description: Selects which image of an image sequence is currently used.

functions.name: GMImage.SetSubRange
functions.description: Sets the Number of images relative to the base image

functions.name: GMImage.SetTileName

functions.name: GMImage.SetType
functions.description: Sets the Image representation type. Changing the type from TrueColorType to GrayscaleType will convert a color picture to grayscale.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: UndefinedType = 0, BilevelType = 1, GrayscaleType = 2, GrayscaleMatteType = 3, PaletteType = 4, PaletteMatteType = 5, TrueColorType = 6, TrueColorMatteType = 7, ColorSeparationType = 8, ColorSeparationMatteType = 9, OptimizeType = 10

examples.label: Change the picture to grayscale.
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 
$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.SetType";$Image; 2); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $Image); 
$Error = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 
$Result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Convert image to 1 bit TIFF:
examples.text_code:
# Load from container
Set Variable [
$Image
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
GraphicsMagick::image
);  ]
# make black and white with threshold
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Threshold
";
$Image
; 127);  ]
# mark as monochrome image
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetMonochrome
";
$Image
; 1);  ]
# set type to 1 for black/white
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("GMImage.SetType";
$Image
; 1);  ]
# set to use TIFF for compression
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetMagick
";
$Image
; "TIFF");  ]
# set to use CCITT 4 Fax compression
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetCompressType
";
$Image
; 4);  ]
# and write to a file
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.WriteToFile
";
$Image
; "/tmp/test.tif");  ]
# cleanup
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$Image
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image);  </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> make black and white with threshold</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Threshold";$Image; 127);  </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> mark as monochrome image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetMonochrome";$Image; 1);  </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set type to 1 for black/white</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetType";$Image; 1);  </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set to use TIFF for compression</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetMagick";$Image; "TIFF");  </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set to use CCITT 4 Fax compression</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetCompressType";$Image; 4);  </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and write to a file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.WriteToFile"; $Image; "/tmp/test.tif");  </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Release";$Image) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Error</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.Shade
functions.description: Shade image using distant light source.

functions.name: GMImage.Sharpen
functions.description: Sharpen pixels in image.

parameters.name: Radius
parameters.description: Optional, The radius of the Gaussian, in pixels, not counting the center pixel.

parameters.name: sigma
parameters.description: Optional, The standard deviation of the Laplacian, in pixels.

examples.label: Applies the sharpen effect to the image:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( 
[
$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$RotateResult = MBS("GMImage.Sharpen"; $Image; 10; 5); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $Image); 
$DestroyResult = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 
$Result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.SharpenChannel
functions.description: Sharpen pixels in image

parameters.name: ChannelType
parameters.description: One of the following values: RedChannel = 1 (RGB Red channel), CyanChannel = 2 (CMYK Cyan channel), GreenChannel = 3 (RGB Green channel), MagentaChannel = 4 (CMYK Magenta channel), BlueChannel = 5 (RGB Blue channel), YellowChannel = 6 (CMYK Yellow channel), OpacityChannel = 7 (Opacity channel), BlackChannel = 8 (CMYK Black (K) channel), MatteChannel = 9 (Same as Opacity channel (deprecated)), AllChannels = 10 (Color channels), GrayChannel = 11 (Color channels represent an intensity.)

parameters.name: Radius
parameters.description: The radius of the Gaussian, in pixels, not counting the center pixel.

parameters.name: sigma
parameters.description: The standard deviation of the Laplacian, in pixels.

functions.name: GMImage.Shave
functions.description: Shave pixels from image edges.

parameters.name: Geometry
parameters.description: The rectangle where to draw. Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200". With an optional offset "100x200+10+20". For percent values, add a percent on the end, for % values add a %: "100x200%!"

$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$ShaveResult = MBS("GMImage.Shave";$Image; "10x10"); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $Image); 
$Error = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 
$Result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.Spread

functions.name: GMImage.Statistics
functions.description: Calculate statistics for an image. ImageStatistics computes common statistics (currently maximum, minimum, mean and standard deviation) for the available image channels. The per-channel values are returned in an ImageStatistics structure as JSON. Statistics are normalized to the range 0.0 to 1.0. Multiply values by 65535 to obtain the statistics in quantum units. Statistics for non-existent channels are set to zero. If all colors have a minimum of zero, you have some black pixels. Same if red, green and blue maximum are all 1, you have some white pixels.

functions.name: GMImage.Stegano
functions.description: Add a digital watermark to the image (based on second image) Use SteganoImage() to hide a digital watermark within the image. Recover the hidden watermark later to prove that the authenticity of an image.  Offset defines the start position within the image to hide the watermark. Warning: This works in 16bit per pixel mode, so the hidden picture is gone if saving as 8 bit picture.

examples.label: Stegano example:
examples.text_code:
# Load both pictures as reference
Set Variable [
$BigImageRef
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Combine Pictures::BigImage
) ]
Set Variable [
$SmallImageRef
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Combine Pictures::SmallImage
) ]
# Add second picture as hidden picture
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.Stegano";
$BigImageRef
;
$SmallImageRef
) ]
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.SetQuality
";
$BigImageRef
; 100 ) ]
# Write to container and free references
Set Field [
Combine Pictures::Result
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
";
$BigImageRef
) ]
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Free
";
$BigImageRef
) ]
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Free
";
$SmallImageRef
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load both pictures as reference</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; Combine Pictures::BigImage) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$BigImageRef</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; Combine Pictures::SmallImage) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$SmallImageRef</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Add second picture as hidden picture</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.Stegano"; $BigImageRef; $SmallImageRef) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Error</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetQuality"; $BigImageRef; 100 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Error</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Write to container and free references</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $BigImageRef ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Combine Pictures" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Free";$BigImageRef) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Error</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Free";$SmallImageRef) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Error</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.Stereo

functions.name: GMImage.Strip
functions.description: Remove all profiles and text attributes from the image.

functions.name: GMImage.StrokePattern
functions.description: Sets the pattern image to use while stroking object outlines. This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 GMImage.Strip - GMImage.Swirl 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: GMImage.Swirl
functions.description: Swirl image (image pixels are rotated by degrees).

examples.label: Does a swirl on the image:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 
$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.Swirl";$Image; 90); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer"; $Image); 
$Error = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 
$Result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.Texture

functions.name: GMImage.Threshold
functions.description: Threshold image channels (below threshold becomes black, above threshold becomes white).

parameters.name: threshold
parameters.description: The range of the threshold parameter is 0 to MaxRGB (65535).

examples.label: Applies a threshold on the image:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 
$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.Threshold";$Image; 127); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer"; $Image); 
$Error = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 
$Result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

; Value:

GMImage.NewFromContainer

);  ]

GMImage.SetCompressType
";
$Image
; 4);  ]
# and write to a file

; Value:

$Image
; "/tmp/test.tif");  ]
# cleanup

; Value:

("
GMImage.Release
";
$Image
) ]

functions.name: GMImage.TotalColors
functions.description: Number of colors in the image This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 GMImage.Threshold - GMImage.Transform 💬 Ask a question or report a problem
functions.result: Returns the number of colors in the image.

functions.name: GMImage.Transform
functions.description: Transform image based on image and crop geometries.

parameters.name: imageGeometry
parameters.description: The rectangle. Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200". With an optional offset "100x200+10+20". For percent values, add a percent on the end, for % values add a %: "100x200%!"

parameters.name: cropGeometry
parameters.description: Optional, The rectangle. Geometry is specified as a width and height "100x200". With an optional offset "100x200+10+20". For percent values, add a percent on the end, for % values add a %: "100x200%!"

functions.name: GMImage.TransformOrigin
functions.description: Origin of coordinate system to use when annotating with text or drawing. See GMImage.TransformReset to reset current transform.

examples.label: Draw rectangle 45° rotated at specific coordinate:

Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.TransformOrigin";
$img
; 50 + 125; 50 + 75 ) ]
# draw rectangle centered there

$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.DrawRectangle
";
$img
; -125; -75; 125; 75) // 250 x 150 ]
# rotate 45°
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.TransformRotation
";
$img
; 45 ) ]
# draw rectangle centered there
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.DrawRectangle
";
$img
; -125; -75; 125; 75) // 250 x 150 ]

functions.name: GMImage.TransformScale

functions.name: GMImage.Translation
functions.description: Apply coordinate translation. Sets the new coordinate origin.

functions.name: GMImage.Transparent
functions.description: Add matte image to image, setting pixels matching color to transparent

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

examples.label: Make a picture transparent:
examples.text_code:
#load a picture
Set Variable [
$image
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Test::BigImage
)]
#white in RGB
Set Variable [
$f
; Value:"COLOR 65535 65535 65535"]
#Replace all white with nearly white
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.ReplaceColor
";
$image
;
$f
; "COLOR 65535 65278 65535"; 0)]
#now flood fill it with 5 as tolerance
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.SetColorFuzz
";
$image
; 5 )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.FloodFillColorXY
";
$image
; 0; 0;
$f
)]
#now set back fuzz and make transparent using white
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.SetColorFuzz
";
$image
; 0 )]
Set Variable [
$imagecolor
; Value:
MBS
( "GMImage.Transparent";
$image
;
$f
)]
#write back to container
Set Field [
Test::Result
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
";
$image
;)]
#cleanup
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$image
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>load a picture</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.NewFromContainer"; Test::BigImage)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>white in RGB</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"COLOR 65535 65535 65535"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$f</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Replace all white with nearly white</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.ReplaceColor"; $image; $f; "COLOR 65535 65278 65535"; 0)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>now flood fill it with 5 as tolerance</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetColorFuzz"; $image; 5 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.FloodFillColorXY"; $image; 0; 0; $f )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>now set back fuzz and make transparent using white</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetColorFuzz"; $image; 0 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.Transparent"; $image; $f)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$imagecolor</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>write back to container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">

functions.name: GMImage.Trim
functions.description: Trim edges that are the background color from the image. Use GMImage.SetColorFuzz to set the fuzzy level. Trim looks on what pixel colors the edges have and checks if there is a smaller rectangle where all pixel outside have same color. See GMImage.BoundingBox for the bounding box of content.

functions.name: GMImage.Unsharpmask
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.Unsharpmask"; ImageRef; Radius; sigma; amount; threshold )
functions.description: Replace image with a sharpened version of the original image using the unsharp mask algorithm.

parameters.name: Radius
parameters.description: the radius of the Gaussian, in pixels, not counting the center pixel.

parameters.name: sigma
parameters.description: the standard deviation of the Gaussian, in pixels.

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: the percentage of the difference between the original and the blur image that is added back into the original. 1 = 100%

parameters.name: threshold
parameters.description: the threshold in pixels needed to apply the diffence amount.

examples.label: Try it:
examples.text_code:
# Load from container
Set Variable [
$ImageRef
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
Test::BigImage
) ]
# apply unsharp mask
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("GMImage.Unsharpmask";
$ImageRef
; 2; 1; 1; ,05) ]
# Store output and release the image
Set Field [
Test::Result
;
MBS
( "
GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
";
$ImageRef
) ]
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Free
";
$ImageRef
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; Test::BigImage) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ImageRef</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> apply unsharp mask</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Unsharpmask"; $ImageRef; 2; 1; 1; ,05) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Store output and release the image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer"; $ImageRef ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Test" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Free";$ImageRef) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Error</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.UnsharpmaskChannel
functions.signature: MBS( "GMImage.UnsharpmaskChannel"; ImageRef; ChannelType; Radius; sigma; amount; threshold )
functions.description: Replace image with a sharpened version of the original image using the unsharp mask algorithm. This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 27th September 2022 GMImage.Unsharpmask - GMImage.Wave 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: ChannelType
parameters.example: One of the following values: RedChannel = 1 (RGB Red channel), CyanChannel = 2 (CMYK Cyan channel), GreenChannel = 3 (RGB Green channel), MagentaChannel = 4 (CMYK Magenta channel), BlueChannel = 5 (RGB Blue channel), YellowChannel = 6 (CMYK Yellow channel), OpacityChannel = 7 (Opacity channel), BlackChannel = 8 (CMYK Black (K) channel), MatteChannel = 9 (Same as Opacity channel (deprecated)), AllChannels = 10 (Color channels), GrayChannel = 11 (Color channels represent an intensity.)

parameters.name: Radius
parameters.description: the radius of the Gaussian, in pixels, not counting the center pixel.

parameters.name: sigma
parameters.description: the standard deviation of the Gaussian, in pixels.

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: the percentage of the difference between the original and the blur image that is added back into the original. 50

parameters.name: threshold
parameters.description: the threshold in pixels needed to apply the diffence amount.

functions.name: GMImage.Wave
functions.description: Map image pixels to a sine wave:

functions.name: GMImage.WriteImages
functions.description: Writes several images to disk. This function can be used to create multi image tiff files. See GMImage.NewImagesFromFile and GMImage.NewImagesFromContainer for reading multi picture files. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: ImageList
parameters.description: The list of image references (numbers returned from the GMImage.New functions) separated by either space or new line character.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: A platform specific filepath where to write the image.

parameters.name: Adjoin
parameters.description: Whether to write one file per image or one image file with all images.

functions.name: GMImage.WriteToBMPContainer
functions.description: Writes the picture in BMP format to a container object which you can store in a container field. For preview a JPEG version is included as well as a file name "image.bmp" and the image size. See also GMImage.WriteToFile , GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer , GMImage.WriteToTiffContainer , GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer and GMImage.WriteToGIFContainer .

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name to use for the container. Default is "image.bmp"

examples.label: Get a container value from the picture in BMP format.
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "GMImage.WriteToBMPContainer";
$MyImageRef
; "logo.bmp" )

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.WriteToContainer
functions.description: Writes images as is into a container. Please set type you want with GMImage.SetMagick function. Similar to other write functions, but allows you to use whatever magick type is set for codec. This function is similar to our specialized functions GMImage.WriteToBMPContainer , GMImage.WriteToGIFContainer , GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer , GMImage.WriteToPDFContainer , GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer and GMImage.WriteToTiffContainer , but you can specify the codec to use. Image is compressed and passed to FileMaker with filename. Then FileMaker (or our plugin for older FileMaker versions before 13) can detect the type from filename and generate required previews. See Plugin.SetPreviewSize to control size of preview picture.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: File name to pass to FileMaker with container.

functions.name: GMImage.WriteToFile
functions.description: Write single image frame to a file. See also GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer , GMImage.WriteToBMPContainer , GMImage.WriteToTiffContainer , GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer and GMImage.WriteToGIFContainer . On Windows the file APIs in GraphicsMagick only do ANSI file paths. MacOS and Linux can do full Unicode. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: A platform specific filepath where to write the image.

examples.label: Convert image to 1 bit TIFF:
examples.text_code:
# Load from container
Set Variable [
$Image
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
GraphicsMagick::image
);  ]
# make black and white with threshold
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Threshold
";
$Image
; 127);  ]
# mark as monochrome image
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetMonochrome
";
$Image
; 1);  ]
# set type to 1 for black/white
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetType
";
$Image
; 1);  ]
# set to use TIFF for compression
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetMagick
";
$Image
; "TIFF");  ]
# set to use CCITT 4 Fax compression
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.SetCompressType
";
$Image
; 4);  ]
# and write to a file
Set Variable [
$Result
; Value:
MBS
("GMImage.WriteToFile";
$Image
; "/tmp/test.tif");  ]
# cleanup
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$Image
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> make black and white with threshold</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Threshold";$Image; 127);  </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> mark as monochrome image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetMonochrome";$Image; 1);  </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set type to 1 for black/white</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetType";$Image; 1);  </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set to use TIFF for compression</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetMagick";$Image; "TIFF");  </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set to use CCITT 4 Fax compression</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.SetCompressType";$Image; 4);  </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and write to a file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.WriteToFile"; $Image; "/tmp/test.tif");  </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>

functions.name: GMImage.WriteToGIFContainer
functions.description: Writes the picture in GIF format to a container value which you can store in a container field. For preview a JPEG version is included as well as a file name "image.gif" and the image size. See also GMImage.WriteToFile , GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer , GMImage.WriteToTiffContainer , GMImage.WriteToBMPContainer and GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer .

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name to use for the container. Default is "image.gif"

examples.label: Get a container value from the picture in GIF format.
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "GMImage.WriteToGIFContainer";
$MyImageRef
; "logo.gif" )
examples.xml_code:

      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.WriteToGIFContainer"; $MyImageRef; "logo.gif" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer
functions.description: Writes the picture in JPEG format to a container value which you can store in a container field. Includes a file name "image.jpg" and the image size. For the quality setting use the GMImage.SetQuality function. For most cases the PNG file format is the better choice as it supports transparency and lossless compression. See also GMImage.WriteToFile , GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer , GMImage.WriteToTiffContainer , GMImage.WriteToBMPContainer and GMImage.WriteToGIFContainer .

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name to use for the container. Default is "image.jpg"

examples.label: Recompresses the image with JPEG compressor and a really bad quality:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 
$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.SetQuality";$Image; 5); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer"; $Image); 
$Error = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 
$Result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.WriteToPDFContainer
functions.description: Writes the picture in PDF format to a container value which you can store in a container field. For preview a JPEG version is included as well as a file name and the image size. Please make sure the DPI value is set via GMImage.SetDensity function as an incorrect value can crop the PDF page. See also GMImage.WriteToFile , GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer , GMImage.WriteToBMPContainer and GMImage.WriteToGIFContainer . If you need more options for PDF file, please use our DynaPDF functions. See Plugin.SetPreviewSize to control size of preview picture.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name to use for the container. Default is "image.pdf"

examples.label: Load image and Save to container as PDF:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 

$Image = MBS("GMImage.NewFromContainer"; GraphicsMagick::image); 
$Result = MBS("GMImage.WriteToPDFContainer"; $Image; "image.pdf"); 

$Error = MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image)
]; 

$Result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer
functions.description: Writes the picture in PNG format to a container value which you can store in a container field. For preview a JPEG version is included as well as a file name "image.png" and the image size. See also GMImage.WriteToFile , GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer , GMImage.WriteToBMPContainer and GMImage.WriteToGIFContainer . Some images have bad ICC profile, so saving them will crash (version 3.4 or lower) or return error (3.5 and newer). In this case, please use MBS(" GMImage.SetICCColorProfile "; ImageRef; "") to remove the profile. See Plugin.SetPreviewSize to control size of preview picture.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name to use for the container. Default is "image.png"
